greek: Καὶ ἦλθον εἰς τὸ πέραν τῆς θαλάσσης εἰς τὴν χώραν τῶν Γερασηνῶν.
bsb_english: On the other side of the sea, they arrived in the region of the Gerasenes.

translation.thai: พระองค์กับพวกสาวกเสด็จข้ามทะเลไปถึงอีกฟากหนึ่ง ในแคว้นเกราซา

key_decisions.thai: พระองค์กับพวกสาวกเสด็จ
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW figs-synecdoche v.21 (applied retroactively): plural verb covers Jesus + the disciples in the boat from Mark 4:36. Thai makes the implicit plural subject explicit ('พระองค์กับพวกสาวก' = Him and the disciples) for clarity. Royal 'เสด็จ' for Jesus' movement.

key_decisions.thai: ข้ามทะเลไปถึงอีกฟากหนึ่ง
key_decisions.rationale: πέραν = 'the other side.' Per uW writing-newevent: this 'and' marks the new event. Thai 'ข้าม...ไปถึงอีกฟากหนึ่ง' (cross-go-arrive at-other side) preserves the geographic shift. Picks up directly from the storm-crossing of Mark 4:35–41.

key_decisions.thai: ในแคว้นเกราซา
key_decisions.rationale: TEXTUAL VARIANT (per uW translate-textvariants): SBLGNT Γερασηνῶν (ℵ*, B, D, lat) vs. Γαδαρηνῶν (A, C, Byz — Matthew) vs. Γεργεσηνῶν (W, fam. 1, fam. 13 — Origen). 'Gerasenes' = people of Gerasa (modern Jerash, ~50km SE of the lake). Geographic difficulty: Gerasa is far from the lakeshore; the swine episode requires a steep cliff into water. Origen later proposed Γέργεσα (modern Kursi) on east shore — fits geography but lacks early MS support. We follow SBLGNT 'เกราซา' (transliterated). Thai 'แคว้น' (region/district) for χώρα. Crossing to a Gentile region is theologically loaded: the first Markan miracle on Gentile soil.

key_decisions.thai: (see notes)
key_decisions.rationale: Γερασηνῶν (SBLGNT) vs. Γαδαρηνῶν vs. Γεργεσηνῶν — three text traditions; we follow SBLGNT. Geographic problem flagged in notes.

translation.notes: TEXTUAL VARIANT: Γερασηνῶν (SBLGNT, ℵ*, B, D) — Gerasa is ~50km from the lake, raising the geographic problem of the herd plunging into the sea. Variants Γαδαρηνῶν (Matt 8:28) and Γεργεσηνῶν (Origen's conjecture from Γέργεσα/Kursi on the east shore, where a cliff exists) attempt to resolve this. Following SBLGNT despite the difficulty. SCENE OPENER: Mark's first NT miracle on Gentile territory — the eastern shore of Galilee was Decapolis, populated by Greco-Romans. The pigs (Levitically unclean) and tomb-dwelling man both signal a domain of impurity. Mark structurally pairs this with the storm-stilling (4:35–41): Jesus tames the chaotic sea, then tames chaos in human form.
translation.thai_summary: ต้นฉบับเก่าที่สุดเรียกเขตนี้ว่าเกราซา (ตามภูเขา) มัทธิว 8:28 เรียกเขตกาดารา ต้นฉบับบางฉบับเรียกเกอร์เกซา (เมืองเล็กริมทะเล) ความสับสนเกิดเพราะเกราซาอยู่ห่างทะเลประมาณ 50 กิโลเมตร — ไม่พอดีกับเรื่องฝูงสุกรโจนลงทะเล แต่ชื่อเมืองหลวงของแคว้นใหญ่มักใช้เรียกทั้งเขตปกครอง

greek: καὶ ἐξελθόντος αὐτοῦ ἐκ τοῦ πλοίου εὐθὺς ὑπήντησεν αὐτῷ ἐκ τῶν μνημείων ἄνθρωπος ἐν πνεύματι ἀκαθάρτῳ,
bsb_english: As soon as Jesus got out of the boat, He was met by a man with an unclean spirit, who was coming from the tombs.

translation.thai: ทันทีที่พระองค์เสด็จขึ้นจากเรือ ก็มีชายคนหนึ่งซึ่งมีวิญญาณโสโครกสิงอยู่ ออกมาจากอุโมงค์ฝังศพ มาพบพระองค์

key_decisions.greek: ἐξελθόντος αὐτοῦ ἐκ τοῦ πλοίου (gen. abs.)
key_decisions.thai: พระองค์เสด็จขึ้นจากเรือ
key_decisions.rationale: Genitive absolute 'when he had gone out of the boat.' Royal 'เสด็จขึ้นจากเรือ' (royal-disembark from boat). Per uW figs-go: 'gone' rather than 'come' from the narrator's perspective on the shore.

key_decisions.thai: ทันทีที่...ก็มี...ออกมา...มาพบ
key_decisions.rationale: Mark's signature εὐθύς. ὑπαντάω = 'come to meet.' Thai 'ทันทีที่' (immediately upon) opens the verse, setting the abrupt pacing typical of Mark.

key_decisions.greek: ἐκ τῶν μνημείων ἄνθρωπος ἐν πνεύματι ἀκαθάρτῳ
key_decisions.thai: ชายคนหนึ่งซึ่งมีวิญญาณโสโครกสิงอยู่ ออกมาจากอุโมงค์ฝังศพ
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW writing-participants: introducing the demon-possessed man as a new character. 'ชายคนหนึ่ง' (a certain man) — natural Thai narrative introduction. 'อุโมงค์ฝังศพ' (tomb-bury-corpse) for μνημεῖον — chamber tombs cut into limestone hillsides, common around the Sea of Galilee. Tombs = ritually impure, unclean spirits = impure — Mark stacks the impurity markers. 'วิญญาณโสโครก' per glossary; 'สิงอยู่' (possess-stay) makes the possession explicit (Greek ἐν πνεύματι uses bare ἐν 'in/with' but the sense is full possession, as vv.3–5 show).

translation.notes: Triple impurity stack: tombs (corpse-impurity) + Gentile region (Levitically unclean territory) + unclean spirit. The man embodies what 1st-century Jewish purity categories separated. Jesus walks in.
translation.thai_summary: ชายคนนั้นอยู่ในความไม่สะอาดตามกฎของโมเสสสามชั้นรวมกัน: (1) อยู่กับศพในอุโมงค์ฝังศพ (2) อยู่ในดินแดนของคนต่างชาติ (3) มีวิญญาณโสโครกสิง ผู้ที่สังคมยิวกันไว้ให้ห่างที่สุด พระเยซูเสด็จเข้าไปหาโดยตรง

greek: ὃς τὴν κατοίκησιν εἶχεν ἐν τοῖς μνήμασιν, καὶ οὐδὲ ἁλύσει οὐκέτι οὐδεὶς ἐδύνατο αὐτὸν δῆσαι
bsb_english: This man had been living in the tombs and could no longer be restrained, even with chains.

translation.thai: ชายคนนั้นอาศัยอยู่ในอุโมงค์ฝังศพ ไม่มีใครสามารถผูกมัดเขาไว้ได้อีกต่อไป แม้ด้วยโซ่ตรวน

key_decisions.thai: อาศัยอยู่
key_decisions.rationale: HAPAX LEGOMENON in NT: κατοίκησις = 'dwelling-place / habitation' (only here). Cognate κατοικέω (verb 'dwell') is common; Mark uses the rarer noun-form. LXX uses κατοίκησις for Israel's settled dwelling (Num 15:2; 1 Sam 27:5). Here the irony: the man's 'dwelling-place' is among the dead. Thai uses verbal form 'อาศัยอยู่' (reside-stay) — the abstract noun would be unnatural in Thai prose. 'อุโมงค์ฝังศพ' consistent with v.2.

key_decisions.thai: ไม่มีใครสามารถ...ได้อีกต่อไป แม้ด้วย
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW figs-doublenegatives: triple negation οὐδέ + οὐκέτι + οὐδεὶς for emphatic intensification (not a Greek logical-positive). Per uW figs-explicit: implies prior failed attempts. Thai uses single strong negation 'ไม่มีใคร...ได้อีกต่อไป' (no one...can...anymore) + 'แม้ด้วย' (even with) preserves the climactic 'not even with chains' force. Background-information construction (uW writing-background) for vv.3–5 — Thai narrative naturally accommodates this.

key_decisions.thai: โซ่ตรวน
key_decisions.rationale: ἅλυσις = chain. Per uW translate-unknown: long flexible metal-link binding. Thai 'โซ่' (chain) standard; 'โซ่ตรวน' (chain-shackle) hendiadys captures the binding-implement category. Anticipates v.4's compound πέδαι + ἁλύσεις.

translation.notes: HAPAX: κατοίκησις (dwelling) — Mark's only NT use. Begins three verses of background description (vv.3–5) about the man's prior condition. The fact that no chain could bind him introduces both his superhuman strength AND his utter isolation — abandoned by community and beyond their power to manage.

greek: διὰ τὸ αὐτὸν πολλάκις πέδαις καὶ ἁλύσεσι δεδέσθαι καὶ διεσπάσθαι ὑπ' αὐτοῦ τὰς ἁλύσεις καὶ τὰς πέδας συντετρῖφθαι, καὶ οὐδεὶς ἴσχυεν αὐτὸν δαμάσαι·
bsb_english: Though he was often bound with chains and shackles, he had broken the chains and shattered the shackles. Now there was no one with the strength to subdue him.

translation.thai: เพราะหลายครั้งที่เขาถูกผูกมัดด้วยตรวนและโซ่ แต่เขาก็กระชากโซ่ขาดและทำลายตรวนแหลกหมด ไม่มีใครมีกำลังพอจะปราบเขาได้

key_decisions.thai: เพราะหลายครั้งที่เขาถูกผูกมัด...แต่เขาก็กระชาก...และทำลาย...แหลกหมด
key_decisions.rationale: Causal articular infinitive with three perfect-tense actions stacking: 'because of having-been-bound...having-been-torn-apart...having-been-shattered.' Per uW grammar-connect-words-phrases v.4: the second καί is contrastive ('but'). Thai chain 'เพราะ...แต่...และ' preserves the causal-contrastive logic. Per uW figs-activepassive: passive διεσπάσθαι/συντετρῖφθαι (chains 'were torn by him') rendered actively in Thai 'เขากระชาก...และทำลาย' (he tore...and shattered) for naturalness.

key_decisions.thai: ตรวน
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW translate-unknown: πέδη = leg-irons/foot-shackles (Latin compes), distinct from hand-chains. Thai 'ตรวน' (foot-shackle) standard; 'โซ่' (chain) for ἁλύσεις. Word-pair preserves the Greek's two-element binding apparatus.

key_decisions.thai: ทำลาย...แหลกหมด
key_decisions.rationale: συντρίβω = 'shatter, smash' (συν- intensive). Perfect passive 'have-been-shattered.' Thai 'แหลกหมด' (utterly-broken-completely) captures the σύν- intensive plus perfective force. Stronger than διασπάω 'tear apart' (chains) — appropriate to the harder iron of leg-shackles.

key_decisions.thai: ปราบ
key_decisions.rationale: δαμάζω = 'tame, subdue, master' (cognate Latin domāre — used of breaking horses, taming wild beasts). Thai 'ปราบ' (subdue/conquer) — the verb suggests this was no ordinary criminal but a wildly uncontrolled force, requiring 'taming' rather than 'arresting.'

translation.notes: Background continues. The vocabulary (δαμάσαι 'to tame') frames the man as wild-beast-like — but δαμάζω in Jas 3:7–8 is also used for the untamable tongue, and only God can tame what humans cannot. Mark sets up that what no chains, no community force can bind — Jesus' word will.

greek: καὶ διὰ παντὸς νυκτὸς καὶ ἡμέρας ἐν τοῖς μνήμασιν καὶ ἐν τοῖς ὄρεσιν ἦν κράζων καὶ κατακόπτων ἑαυτὸν λίθοις.
bsb_english: Night and day in the tombs and in the mountains he kept crying out and cutting himself with stones.

translation.thai: ทั้งคืนทั้งวันเขาอยู่ในอุโมงค์ฝังศพและบนภูเขา ร้องตะโกนและเอาหินกรีดเนื้อตัวเอง

key_decisions.thai: ทั้งคืนทั้งวัน
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW figs-idiom: 'throughout every night and day' = continuously, all the time. Thai 'ทั้งคืนทั้งวัน' (whole-night-whole-day) idiomatic for continual activity. Note Greek order night-then-day (Jewish reckoning).

key_decisions.thai: ร้องตะโกนและเอาหินกรีดเนื้อตัวเอง
key_decisions.rationale: Periphrastic imperfect: εἰμί + present participle = ongoing/habitual action. Thai's natural continuous frame ('ทั้งคืนทั้งวัน...อยู่...ร้อง...กรีด') captures the durative force without needing 'กำลัง.' The man's habitual condition.

key_decisions.thai: เอาหินกรีดเนื้อตัวเอง
key_decisions.rationale: HAPAX LEGOMENON in NT: κατακόπτω = 'cut down, cut to pieces' (κατά intensive + κόπτω). LXX uses for striking/cutting flesh in pagan ritual mourning (1 Kgs 18:28 — Baal prophets gashing themselves). Mark may evoke that pagan-cultic background here in Gentile territory. Thai 'กรีด' (slash/score) + 'เนื้อตัวเอง' (own flesh) — 'กรีด' is the Thai verb for sustained cutting/scoring (knife on flesh, fingernail on screen). Made explicit 'เนื้อ' (flesh/body) — Greek λίθοις ('with stones') instrumental dative shifted to natural Thai construction 'เอาหิน...กรีด' (take stones...slash).

translation.notes: HAPAX: κατακόπτω (self-laceration). The pagan-mourning resonance of LXX 1 Kgs 18:28 may be deliberate — the man enacts a cult of self-destruction in a tomb-temple. Three signs of his condition: place (tombs/mountains = wild, isolated, dead), action (crying out — primal sound), self-violence (cutting). The total picture: a man whose own body is the site of the demonic war.

greek: καὶ ἰδὼν τὸν Ἰησοῦν ἀπὸ μακρόθεν ἔδραμεν καὶ προσεκύνησεν αὐτόν,
bsb_english: When the man saw Jesus from a distance, he ran and fell on his knees before Him.

translation.thai: เมื่อเขาเห็นพระเยซูแต่ไกล เขาก็วิ่งเข้ามาก้มกราบลงเฉพาะพระพักตร์

key_decisions.thai: เมื่อเขาเห็นพระเยซูแต่ไกล
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW figs-explicit: this resumes the foreground after vv.3–5 background. Thai 'เมื่อ...แต่ไกล' (when...from afar) preserves the spatial-temporal setup. The narrator's camera switches back to live action.

key_decisions.thai: วิ่งเข้ามา
key_decisions.rationale: τρέχω = run. Thai 'วิ่งเข้ามา' (run-come-toward) directional, capturing the demoniac's paradoxical rush toward Jesus.

key_decisions.thai: ก้มกราบลงเฉพาะพระพักตร์
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW translate-symaction: προσκυνέω = bow down/prostrate (lit. 'kiss toward'). In secular use it can mean either reverent worship or simple prostration before a superior. Here ambiguous: the demon recognizes Jesus' superior power but the act is hostile, not worshipful (cf. v.7's defiance). Thai 'ก้มกราบลง' (bow-prostrate-down) preserves the physical action; 'เฉพาะพระพักตร์' (in the royal-presence-of) preserves the royal register for Jesus' presence — even the hostile demon must use this register before divine presence.

translation.notes: PARADOX: the demon-possessed man runs to Jesus. Mark juxtaposes the man's wild flight (vv.3–5) with sudden recognition. The demon knows what the townspeople and disciples don't — Jesus' identity. προσκυνέω here is involuntary submission of an inferior power, not worship.

greek: καὶ κράξας φωνῇ μεγάλῃ λέγει· Τί ἐμοὶ καὶ σοί, Ἰησοῦ υἱὲ τοῦ θεοῦ τοῦ ὑψίστου; ὁρκίζω σε τὸν θεόν, μή με βασανίσῃς.
bsb_english: And he shouted in a loud voice, "What do You want with me, Jesus, Son of the Most High God? I beg You before God not to torture me!"

translation.thai: เขาตะโกนด้วยเสียงอันดังว่า 'เรื่องอะไรระหว่างข้ากับท่าน พระเยซู พระบุตรของพระเจ้าผู้สูงสุด? ข้าขอสาบานต่อท่านในนามพระเจ้าว่า อย่าทรมานข้าเลย!'

key_decisions.thai: ตะโกนด้วยเสียงอันดัง
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW figs-idiom: 'crying out with a loud voice' = yelled loudly. Thai 'ตะโกนด้วยเสียงอันดัง' (shout-with voice loud) standard biblical phrasing. Same construction recurs at 5:7, 15:34, 15:37.

key_decisions.thai: เรื่องอะไรระหว่างข้ากับท่าน
key_decisions.rationale: HEBRAISM (LXX): mah-li-walak (cf. Judg 11:12; 2 Sam 16:10; 1 Kgs 17:18; Mark 1:24). Per uW figs-rquestion + figs-idiom: a defensive challenge — 'what business do you have with me?' / 'why are you involving yourself with me?' Thai 'เรื่องอะไรระหว่างข้ากับท่าน' (what-matter between-I-and-you) preserves the idiomatic ring. Demon to Jesus uses 'ข้า' (humble-direct, distancing) and 'ท่าน' (respectful but cold) — same register as Mark 1:24 per glossary precedent.

key_decisions.greek: Ἰησοῦ υἱὲ τοῦ θεοῦ τοῦ ὑψίστου
key_decisions.thai: พระเยซู พระบุตรของพระเจ้าผู้สูงสุด
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW guidelines-sonofgodprinciples: 'Son of the Most High God' is a divine-relational title. ὕψιστος = 'Most High' (LXX equivalent of Hebrew Elyon, an ancient divine name — cf. Gen 14:18–22 with Melchizedek). The demon names Jesus more accurately than humans yet in this text — knowledge without submission. Thai 'พระบุตรของพระเจ้าผู้สูงสุด' (Holy-Son-of-God-the-Most-High). Note: even as the demon resists, Mark requires the name 'พระเยซู' to retain its 'พระ' honorific — the narrator's text honors Jesus throughout, even in demonic speech.

key_decisions.thai: ข้าขอสาบานต่อท่านในนามพระเจ้า
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW writing-oathformula: ὁρκίζω + double accusative 'I adjure you by God.' The demon attempts to put Jesus under oath — a manipulative reversal (a demon invoking God against God's Son). Thai 'ข้าขอสาบานต่อท่านในนามพระเจ้า' (I-pledge-oath to-you in-name-of-God) preserves the formal oath-formula. The demon uses standard religious speech-formulas as a defensive tactic.

key_decisions.thai: อย่าทรมานข้าเลย
key_decisions.rationale: βασανίζω = 'torment, torture' (originally 'to test by touchstone'). NT-significant verb for eschatological judgment (Rev 14:10, 20:10; cf. Matt 8:29 par. — 'before the appointed time'). Thai 'อย่าทรมาน' (don't torment) direct prohibition; 'เลย' (at all/please) softens to plea. The demon recognizes Jesus' authority to judge and pleads for delay.

key_decisions.thai: ข้า...ท่าน
key_decisions.rationale: Per glossary precedent (Mark 1:24): demon addresses Jesus with hostile/distancing register. 'ข้า' (humble-direct, fits a lower adversary forced to acknowledge superior) + 'ท่าน' (respectful but kept at arm's length — not the worshipful 'พระองค์' that humans use). Avoids ราชาศัพท์.

translation.notes: HEBRAISM: 'What to me and to you' (mah-li-walak) — defensive challenge. ESCHATOLOGICAL VOCABULARY: βασανίζω is the language of final judgment; the parallel in Matt 8:29 spells this out ('before the appointed time'). The demon knows the eschaton is upon him in Jesus' presence. THEOLOGICAL CRUX: a demon utters a Christological confession — but knowledge alone is not salvation (cf. Jas 2:19). REGISTER: Demon to Jesus = ข้า/ท่าน, refusing the worshipful พระองค์/ข้าพระองค์ used by faithful humans (Mark 1:40 leper).

greek: ἔλεγεν γὰρ αὐτῷ· Ἔξελθε τὸ πνεῦμα τὸ ἀκάθαρτον ἐκ τοῦ ἀνθρώπου.
bsb_english: For Jesus had already declared, "Come out of this man, you unclean spirit!"

translation.thai: เพราะพระองค์ได้ตรัสกับมันว่า 'จงออกมาจากชายคนนี้ เจ้าวิญญาณโสโครก!'

key_decisions.thai: เพราะพระองค์ได้ตรัสกับมัน
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW grammar-connect-logic-result: γάρ supplies the explanation for the demon's outburst in v.7 — Jesus had been commanding the spirit to leave. Imperfect tense suggests sustained / repeated command (or anterior-to-the-cry). Thai 'เพราะ...ได้ตรัส' (because...had-spoken) flashback frame. Royal 'ตรัส' for Jesus.

key_decisions.thai: จงออกมาจากชายคนนี้
key_decisions.rationale: Standard exorcism command. Thai 'จงออกมา' (imperative-go-out-toward) preserves the imperative force. 'ชายคนนี้' (this man) makes ἀνθρώπου specific — note Jesus addresses the spirit BUT speaks of the man as a person, distinguishing them.

key_decisions.thai: เจ้าวิญญาณโสโครก
key_decisions.rationale: Vocative-style nominative addressing the spirit directly. Thai 'เจ้า' (intimate-direct, marking superior addressing inferior) + 'วิญญาณโสโครก' per glossary. Jesus' register: divine authority addressing demonic adversary — uses 'เจ้า' (the imperious 'you'), refusing any honorific. Parallel to Mark 1:25 ('φιμώθητι')."

translation.notes: Mark interrupts the demon's speech to give the back-story: Jesus had already issued the command before the demon's outburst. This explains the demon's defensive posture in v.7 — it is responding to an authoritative dispossession order. Notable: Jesus addresses 'the unclean spirit' as a separate entity from 'the man' — distinguishing victim from possessor. The exorcism formula matches Mark 1:25.

greek: καὶ ἐπηρώτα αὐτόν· Τί ὄνομά σοι; καὶ λέγει αὐτῷ· Λεγιὼν ὄνομά μοι, ὅτι πολλοί ἐσμεν·
bsb_english: "What is your name?" Jesus asked. "My name is Legion," he replied, "for we are many."

translation.thai: พระองค์ตรัสถามมันว่า 'เจ้าชื่ออะไร?' มันทูลตอบว่า 'ข้าชื่อเลกิโอน เพราะพวกข้ามีจำนวนมาก'

key_decisions.thai: ตรัสถาม
key_decisions.rationale: Imperfect — perhaps repeated questioning or narrative-progressive. Thai royal 'ตรัสถาม' (royal-ask). Jesus asks a name as part of the exorcism — name = identity = the handle by which a power is engaged.

key_decisions.thai: มันทูลตอบ
key_decisions.rationale: Historic present (per uW v.9). 'ทูล' is the deferential speech-verb a subordinate uses when addressing a superior — here ironic: the demon must answer with the verbal form of submission even as it resists. The demon's answer is forced, not free.

key_decisions.thai: ข้าชื่อเลกิโอน
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW translate-names: λεγιών (Latin legio) = a Roman legion, ~6,000 soldiers. Thai 'เลกิโอน' (transliteration; standard in Thai Christian Bible tradition). Per uW alternative ('Army', 'Battalion'): we keep the transliteration because (a) it preserves the proper-name function the text gives it, (b) Thai readers familiar with Roman-era vocabulary recognize 'เลกิโอน,' (c) the Latin loanword in a Greek text is itself a feature — Mark preserves the foreign military term, evoking Roman occupation. POLITICAL RESONANCE: the demonic legion in pig-form drowning in the sea may have echoed for early readers as occupation-imagery (cf. Pharaoh's army at the Red Sea).

key_decisions.thai: เพราะพวกข้ามีจำนวนมาก
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW grammar-connect-logic-result + figs-explicit: shift from singular 'my' to plural 'we' — multiple demons speaking through one mouth. Thai 'พวกข้า' (plural humble-self) preserves this number-shift; 'มีจำนวนมาก' (have great-number) for πολλοί. Crucial setup for vv.10–13.

translation.notes: NUMBER SHIFT: μοι (sg.) → ἐσμεν (pl.). The demon-speech alternates singular/plural throughout vv.6–13 because the legion speaks corporately through one mouth. Per uW chapter intro on 'How to refer to the demons' — Mark deliberately keeps this fluid. ROMAN ECHO: λεγιών = Roman military unit. The Decapolis was Roman-occupied; the legion-imagery may have political-spiritual overtones for first readers. The unclean spirits drowning in the sea via unclean pigs is Mark's cosmic-political tableau: occupying-force destroyed in waters — Exodus typology.
translation.thai_summary: 'เลเกอย' เป็นคำทหารโรมัน = กองพันประมาณ 6,000 คน นัยทางการเมือง: ชาวยิวในพระกิตติคุณใกล้ยุคของการก่อกบฏ (ค.ศ. 66-70) เห็นทั้งปีศาจและทหารโรมันเป็นกองกำลังยึดครองดินแดนของพระเจ้า พระเยซูทรงขับทั้งสองออก

greek: καὶ παρεκάλει αὐτὸν πολλὰ ἵνα μὴ αὐτὰ ἀποστείλῃ ἔξω τῆς χώρας.
bsb_english: And he begged Jesus repeatedly not to send them out of that region.

translation.thai: มันวิงวอนพระองค์อย่างยิ่ง อย่าทรงขับพวกมันออกไปจากแคว้นนั้น

key_decisions.thai: วิงวอน...อย่างยิ่ง
key_decisions.rationale: παρακαλέω 'urge, beg, entreat' — imperfect indicates sustained begging. πολλά = adverbial 'much, repeatedly.' Thai 'วิงวอน' (entreat/plead) standard Thai for desperate request; 'อย่างยิ่ง' (very much / earnestly) preserves the intensifier.

key_decisions.thai: ทรงขับพวกมันออกไปจากแคว้นนั้น
key_decisions.rationale: ἀποστέλλω = 'send away, send out.' The demons want to stay local — perhaps because exile to ἄβυσσος (the abyss; cf. Luke 8:31) is the dreaded alternative, or because they're territorial spirits. Royal 'ทรงขับ' (royal-cast-out) for Jesus' action; 'พวกมัน' (them, plural) consistent with v.9's number-shift. 'แคว้น' for χώρα consistent with v.1.

translation.notes: Per uW figs-quotations alternative: could be rendered as direct quotation. We keep indirect to match the Greek's grammatical form. The demons' attachment to 'this region' is theologically interesting — pre-modern cosmologies often associated specific spirits with specific places (Deut 32:8 LXX). Their preference is to remain in Decapolis (Gentile) rather than be exiled.

greek: ἦν δὲ ἐκεῖ πρὸς τῷ ὄρει ἀγέλη χοίρων μεγάλη βοσκομένη·
bsb_english: There on the nearby hillside a large herd of pigs was feeding.

translation.thai: บนเนินเขาที่นั่น มีฝูงสุกรฝูงใหญ่กำลังหากินอยู่

key_decisions.thai: บน...ที่นั่น มี
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW writing-background v.11: δέ marks scene-setting background, not story-progression. Thai 'มี' (there is/was) sets the scene without driving the action — naturally reads as descriptive.

key_decisions.thai: บนเนินเขา
key_decisions.rationale: πρός + dat. = 'near, by.' τὸ ὄρος = mountain/hillside. Thai 'เนินเขา' (hillside) for the gentler topography implied. Sets up v.13's κρημνός (cliff).

key_decisions.thai: ฝูงสุกรฝูงใหญ่
key_decisions.rationale: ἀγέλη = herd. χοῖρος = pig. Levitically unclean (Lev 11:7); their presence confirms Gentile region. Thai 'สุกร' (pig — formal/biblical register; everyday Thai uses 'หมู' but biblical translations use 'สุกร'). 'ฝูงใหญ่' (great herd) — 2,000 per v.13.

key_decisions.thai: กำลังหากินอยู่
key_decisions.rationale: βόσκω 'feed/graze' (mid./pass.). Thai 'กำลังหากิน' (currently-finding-food) + 'อยู่' (continuous particle) preserves the ongoing-feeding scene.

translation.notes: PIGS = Levitically unclean animal (Lev 11:7; Deut 14:8) — confirms Gentile setting. The juxtaposition is loaded: unclean spirits + unclean animals + tomb-impurity all converging.

greek: καὶ παρεκάλεσαν αὐτὸν λέγοντες· Πέμψον ἡμᾶς εἰς τοὺς χοίρους, ἵνα εἰς αὐτοὺς εἰσέλθωμεν.
bsb_english: So the demons begged Jesus, "Send us to the pigs, so that we may enter them."

translation.thai: พวกมันจึงวิงวอนพระองค์ว่า 'ขอทรงโปรดส่งพวกข้าเข้าไปในฝูงสุกร เพื่อพวกข้าจะได้เข้าสิงพวกมัน'

key_decisions.thai: พวกมันจึงวิงวอน
key_decisions.rationale: Aorist (vs. imperfect παρεκάλει v.10) marks the climactic culmination of the begging into a specific request. Plural verb confirms many speakers. Thai 'พวกมัน' (them — plural marker) consistent.

key_decisions.thai: ขอทรงโปรดส่งพวกข้าเข้าไปในฝูงสุกร
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW figs-imperative: imperative softened to polite request. Thai 'ขอทรงโปรด' (request-royal-graciously) marks the demon's deference to Jesus' superior power even while making a request — 'please' formula. Per uW figs-exclusive: 'us/we' = demons only (not Jesus or disciples). Thai 'พวกข้า' first-person-plural-exclusive contextually clear.

key_decisions.thai: เข้าสิงพวกมัน
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW figs-explicit: 'enter into them' = enter and possess. Thai 'เข้าสิง' (enter-possess) standard biblical idiom for demonic possession (cf. v.2 'สิงอยู่'). 'พวกมัน' refers back to the pigs.

translation.notes: The demons' request implies they need an embodied host — cannot be 'free-floating.' Why pigs? Several theories: (1) pigs are the nearest available embodied targets; (2) pigs are unclean — fitting hosts for unclean spirits; (3) Jesus uses the demons' request to perform an unmistakable public exorcism (the 2,000-pig drowning is visible evidence). Mark gives no commentary — the reader must infer.

greek: καὶ ἐπέτρεψεν αὐτοῖς. καὶ ἐξελθόντα τὰ πνεύματα τὰ ἀκάθαρτα εἰσῆλθον εἰς τοὺς χοίρους, καὶ ὥρμησεν ἡ ἀγέλη κατὰ τοῦ κρημνοῦ εἰς τὴν θάλασσαν, ὡς δισχίλιοι, καὶ ἐπνίγοντο ἐν τῇ θαλάσσῃ.
bsb_english: He gave them permission, and the unclean spirits came out and went into the pigs, and the herd of about two thousand rushed down the steep bank into the sea and drowned in the water.

translation.thai: พระองค์ก็ทรงอนุญาต บรรดาวิญญาณโสโครกก็ออกจากชายนั้นแล้วเข้าสิงสุกร ฝูงสุกรประมาณสองพันตัวก็วิ่งกระโจนลงจากหน้าผาสู่ทะเล และจมน้ำตายในทะเลนั้น

key_decisions.thai: ทรงอนุญาต
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW figs-explicit: Jesus permitted them. Royal 'ทรงอนุญาต' (royal-permit). Mark is laconic — no editorial comment on the morality. The verb itself is neutral: he allowed it. Theological discussion belongs in commentary, not translation.

key_decisions.thai: ออก...แล้วเข้าสิง
key_decisions.rationale: Two aorists in sequence: out-of-man, into-pigs. Thai 'ออกจากชาย...แล้วเข้าสิง' (exit man...then enter-possess) makes the man explicit (per uW figs-explicit alternate: 'having come out of the man'). 'สิง' (possess) preserves the same possessive verb as v.12.

key_decisions.greek: ὥρμησεν...κατὰ τοῦ κρημνοῦ εἰς τὴν θάλασσαν
key_decisions.thai: วิ่งกระโจนลงจากหน้าผาสู่ทะเล
key_decisions.rationale: ὁρμάω = 'rush impetuously, charge.' κατά + gen. = 'down from.' κρημνός = cliff/precipice (Mark only here in NT among synoptics). Thai 'วิ่งกระโจน' (run-leap) preserves the violent sudden motion; 'ลงจากหน้าผา' (down-from cliff-face) standard Thai for cliff-edge; 'สู่ทะเล' (toward sea) directional. Vivid scene — the pigs do something pigs would not normally do.

key_decisions.thai: ประมาณสองพันตัว
key_decisions.rationale: HAPAX in Mark: δισχίλιοι = 2,000 (only here in Mark; rare elsewhere — Acts 27:14, Rev 11:3). Thai 'ประมาณสองพันตัว' (approximately 2,000 head) — 'ตัว' is the standard Thai counter for animals. The number is a parenthetical aside — narrator's later reckoning, signaled by ὡς ('about'). The number contributes to the Roman-legion typology: a legion (~6,000) split among 2,000 pigs = multiple demons per pig.

key_decisions.thai: จมน้ำตาย
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW figs-activepassive: passive πνίγω 'be choked, drown.' Thai 'จมน้ำตาย' (sink-water-die) idiomatic — 'จม' (sink) + 'น้ำ' (water) + 'ตาย' (die) compresses the Greek descriptive into one Thai construction. Imperfect = 'were drowning' (the action was unfolding). Translates the result as completed for Thai naturalness.

translation.notes: HAPAX (in Mark): δισχίλιοι (2,000). EXODUS TYPOLOGY: occupying force (legion) destroyed in the sea — Pharaoh's army drowned (Exod 14:28, ὕδατι ἐσκέπασεν αὐτούς). Mark may be drawing the typology for first-readers: Jesus liberates God's land from foreign occupation, demonic and (by implication) political. ECONOMIC LOSS: the loss of 2,000 pigs would be devastating — explains the townspeople's request in v.17 that Jesus leave. The healed man's restoration costs them their livelihood.
translation.thai_summary: ภาพฝูงสุกรจมน้ำตายในทะเลเป็นภาพเอกโซดัส: กองทัพฟาโรห์จมน้ำในทะเลแดง (อพยพ 14:28) มาระโกอาจจงใจให้ผู้อ่านเห็นว่าพระเยซูทรงปลดปล่อยดินแดนจากกองกำลังยึดครอง — ทั้งปีศาจและ (โดยนัย) โรมัน

greek: Καὶ οἱ βόσκοντες αὐτοὺς ἔφυγον καὶ ἀπήγγειλαν εἰς τὴν πόλιν καὶ εἰς τοὺς ἀγρούς· καὶ ἦλθον ἰδεῖν τί ἐστιν τὸ γεγονός.
bsb_english: Those tending the pigs ran off and reported this in the town and countryside, and the people went out to see what had happened.

translation.thai: บรรดาคนเลี้ยงสุกรก็หนีไป และนำเรื่องไปเล่าทั้งในเมืองและตามชนบท ผู้คนก็พากันออกมาดูว่าเกิดอะไรขึ้น

key_decisions.thai: บรรดาคนเลี้ยงสุกร
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW writing-participants: 'the ones feeding them' — pig-herders, new characters. Thai 'บรรดาคนเลี้ยงสุกร' (the various pig-feeders) plural-collective. Their fleeing is natural — they'd witnessed a supernatural event AND lost their employer's herd.

key_decisions.greek: εἰς τὴν πόλιν καὶ εἰς τοὺς ἀγρούς
key_decisions.thai: ทั้งในเมืองและตามชนบท
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW figs-merism: city + countryside = the whole region. Thai 'ทั้ง...และ' (both...and) merism preserved. Not a hendiadys — keeping both terms gives the report the breadth Mark intends. Per uW figs-explicit: city = Gerasa (or whichever locale).

key_decisions.thai: พากันออกมาดู
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW writing-pronouns: 'they' = the people of the region. Thai 'พากันออกมาดู' (gather-and-go-out-to-see) — 'พากัน' (collectively) preserves the social-collective movement of the curious crowd.

translation.notes: Pig-herders flee → spread word → townspeople come out. Mark sets up the audience for v.15's reveal.

greek: καὶ ἔρχονται πρὸς τὸν Ἰησοῦν, καὶ θεωροῦσιν τὸν δαιμονιζόμενον καθήμενον ἱματισμένον καὶ σωφρονοῦντα, τὸν ἐσχηκότα τὸν λεγιῶνα, καὶ ἐφοβήθησαν.
bsb_english: When they came to Jesus, they saw the man who had been possessed by the legion of demons sitting there, clothed and in his right mind; and they were afraid.

translation.thai: พวกเขามาถึงพระเยซู และเห็นชายที่เคยถูกผีสิงนั่งอยู่ นุ่งห่มเสื้อผ้าและมีสติสัมปชัญญะดี เป็นคนเดียวกับที่เคยถูกเลกิโอนสิง พวกเขาก็กลัว

key_decisions.thai: พวกเขามาถึง...และเห็น
key_decisions.rationale: Two historic presents in sequence (per uW v.15): vivid narrative-present. Thai uses simple narrative past 'มาถึง...เห็น' since Thai relies on context for tense; the dramatic effect of the historic-present is preserved by the narrative pacing.

key_decisions.thai: ชายที่เคยถูกผีสิง
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW figs-explicit + figs-activepassive: 'the demon-possessed (one)' here refers to the man who used to be possessed. Thai 'เคย' (formerly/used to) signals past-state; 'ผีสิง' (ghost/spirit-possess) — note: 'ผี' here is more colloquial than 'วิญญาณโสโครก,' reflecting the perspective of the townspeople who saw demonic possession from outside. The shift works in Thai: narrator's earlier 'วิญญาณโสโครก' is technical/biblical; townspeople-eye-view 'ผีสิง' is vernacular.

key_decisions.thai: นั่งอยู่ นุ่งห่มเสื้อผ้าและมีสติสัมปชัญญะดี
key_decisions.rationale: Three participles describing the dramatic transformation. ἱματισμένον (perf. pass.) 'clothed' — Mark hadn't told us he was naked, but the implication of vv.3–5 (tomb-dweller, self-cutter) is consistent with nakedness; the parallel in Luke 8:27 makes it explicit. σωφρονέω = 'be of sound mind' (per uW figs-idiom). Thai 'นั่งอยู่' (sitting), 'นุ่งห่มเสื้อผ้า' (wearing-clothes — paired Thai 'นุ่ง'+'ห่ม' for both lower-and-upper garments), 'มีสติสัมปชัญญะดี' (have-mind-consciousness-good — the standard Thai phrase for sanity / mental composure, used in legal and medical contexts). Three signs reverse the earlier scene: posture (sitting vs. wandering), appearance (clothed vs. naked), and mind (sound vs. crying out).

key_decisions.thai: เป็นคนเดียวกับที่เคยถูกเลกิโอนสิง
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW figs-idiom: 'the one having had the legion' = the one possessed by the legion. Thai 'เป็นคนเดียวกับ' (is-the-same-person-as) makes the identification explicit; 'ถูกเลกิโอนสิง' (was-by-Legion-possessed) preserves the Legion-name as proper-noun. NB: 'เลกิโอน' here functions as a near-name (per uW translate-unknown) — once introduced as a name in v.9, subsequent uses retain that quality.

key_decisions.thai: พวกเขาก็กลัว
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW figs-explicit: they were afraid of what else such a powerful one might do. Thai 'กลัว' (afraid) bare — Mark himself doesn't elaborate; we don't either, letting the next verses unfold the response.

translation.notes: DRAMATIC REVERSAL: the townspeople see the same man, transformed. Three signs (sitting, clothed, sane) invert vv.3–5 (wild, naked, crying). Their response is FEAR (not joy) — this man's restoration is more frightening than his madness, because it reveals a power they cannot manage. The same root reaction will recur in 4:41 and elsewhere: encounter with Jesus' divine power produces holy dread.

greek: καὶ διηγήσαντο αὐτοῖς οἱ ἰδόντες πῶς ἐγένετο τῷ δαιμονιζομένῳ καὶ περὶ τῶν χοίρων.
bsb_english: Those who had seen it described what had happened to the demon-possessed man and also to the pigs.

translation.thai: บรรดาผู้ที่เห็นเหตุการณ์ก็เล่าให้พวกเขาฟัง ว่าเกิดอะไรขึ้นกับชายที่ถูกผีสิงและกับฝูงสุกร

key_decisions.thai: เล่าให้...ฟัง
key_decisions.rationale: διηγέομαι = 'recount in detail, narrate.' Thai 'เล่าให้...ฟัง' (recount-give-listen) standard for first-hand-account narration. Stronger than mere ἀπαγγέλλω 'report' (v.14).

key_decisions.thai: บรรดาผู้ที่เห็นเหตุการณ์
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW figs-explicit: those who had seen what happened (eyewitnesses — the pig-herders). 'บรรดาผู้ที่เห็นเหตุการณ์' (the-various-ones-who-saw-the-event) — 'เหตุการณ์' (event) supplied for clarity, as Greek bare 'οἱ ἰδόντες' would be opaque in Thai.

key_decisions.greek: πῶς ἐγένετο τῷ δαιμονιζομένῳ καὶ περὶ τῶν χοίρων
key_decisions.thai: ว่าเกิดอะไรขึ้นกับชายที่ถูกผีสิงและกับฝูงสุกร
key_decisions.rationale: Two indirect-question objects: 'how it happened to the demon-possessed (one)' + 'concerning the pigs.' Thai 'ว่าเกิดอะไรขึ้นกับ...และกับ' (that-happened-what-with...and-with) flattens both into a single Thai indirect-question. Note 'ผีสิง' (vernacular 'demon-possessed') consistent with v.15's townspeople-eye-view.

translation.notes: Eyewitnesses fill in the story for the newcomers. The two objects (man + pigs) prepare for the people's mixed reaction in v.17 — gratitude for the man's healing competing with grief over the pig loss.

greek: καὶ ἤρξαντο παρακαλεῖν αὐτὸν ἀπελθεῖν ἀπὸ τῶν ὁρίων αὐτῶν.
bsb_english: And the people began to beg Jesus to leave their region.

translation.thai: พวกเขาก็เริ่มอ้อนวอนพระองค์ให้เสด็จออกไปจากเขตแดนของพวกเขา

key_decisions.thai: เริ่มอ้อนวอน
key_decisions.rationale: ἄρχομαι + inf. = 'begin to.' Thai 'เริ่มอ้อนวอน' (begin-to-plead). 'อ้อนวอน' is gentler than 'วิงวอน' — appropriate to townspeople vs. demonic begging in vv.10, 12.

key_decisions.thai: เสด็จออกไปจากเขตแดนของพวกเขา
key_decisions.rationale: ὅριον = boundary, region. Royal 'เสด็จออกไป' (royal-depart) for Jesus. Thai 'เขตแดน' (territory/boundary) preserves the boundary-marker sense — they want him outside their borders, not just out of sight. Per uW figs-quotations alternative: could be direct quote 'Please depart.' Indirect form preserved per Greek.

translation.notes: TRAGIC IRONY: the townspeople ask the only one who can heal them to leave. The economic loss (pigs) and the holy fear (v.15) outweigh any desire for further blessing. Jesus is treated as a disturbance to the local order. This contrasts sharply with Mark 1:32–34 (Capernaum welcomes him at sundown) — Jewish vs. Gentile reception. But Mark balances this: vv.18–20 show that one Gentile soul does receive him.
translation.thai_summary: ความย้อนแย้งที่เจ็บปวด: ชาวเมืองขอให้พระเยซูผู้สามารถรักษาทุกคนได้เสด็จออกไป การสูญเสียเศรษฐกิจ (สุกร) และความกลัวสิ่งที่ไม่เข้าใจมีน้ำหนักมากกว่าความต้องการพระพร ตรงข้ามอย่างสิ้นเชิงกับเมืองคาเปอรนาอุม (1:32-34) ที่ต้อนรับพระองค์

greek: καὶ ἐμβαίνοντος αὐτοῦ εἰς τὸ πλοῖον παρεκάλει αὐτὸν ὁ δαιμονισθεὶς ἵνα μετ' αὐτοῦ ᾖ.
bsb_english: As He was getting into the boat, the man who had been possessed by the demons begged to go with Him.

translation.thai: ขณะที่พระองค์กำลังเสด็จลงเรือ ชายที่เคยถูกผีสิงก็วิงวอนพระองค์ ขอให้อยู่กับพระองค์ด้วย

key_decisions.thai: ขณะที่พระองค์กำลังเสด็จลงเรือ
key_decisions.rationale: Genitive absolute 'while he was embarking.' Thai 'ขณะที่...กำลังเสด็จลง' (while...currently royal-descending) preserves the temporal contemporaneity — Jesus is in the act of leaving as the request comes.

key_decisions.thai: ชายที่เคยถูกผีสิง
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW figs-explicit + figs-activepassive: aorist passive 'the one having been demon-possessed' = the formerly possessed man. Thai 'เคยถูกผีสิง' (formerly-was-by-spirit-possessed) consistent with v.15. The textual identity of this transformed man as the same individual is critical to the narrative.

key_decisions.thai: วิงวอนพระองค์ ขอให้อยู่กับพระองค์ด้วย
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW figs-idiom: 'be with him' = become a disciple, follow him wherever he goes. Echoes Mark 3:14 (Jesus appointed the Twelve 'ἵνα ὦσιν μετ' αὐτοῦ' — 'that they might be with him'). The healed man wants to join the disciple-circle. Thai 'ขอให้อยู่กับพระองค์ด้วย' (request to-stay with him also) preserves this discipleship-request sense. 'วิงวอน' (urgent plea) for παρεκάλει — note the same verb the demons used (v.10), but now in the man's restored voice.

translation.notes: ECHO of Mark 3:14: 'ἵνα ὦσιν μετ' αὐτοῦ' — to be with him is the basic disciple-mark. The healed Gentile asks for that vocation. Jesus' response (v.19) reframes: his disciple-vocation is to stay home and witness in his own land. STRUCTURAL: the demoniac becomes the first Gentile evangelist.

greek: καὶ οὐκ ἀφῆκεν αὐτόν, ἀλλὰ λέγει αὐτῷ· Ὕπαγε εἰς τὸν οἶκόν σου πρὸς τοὺς σούς, καὶ ἀπάγγειλον αὐτοῖς ὅσα ὁ κύριός σοι πεποίηκεν καὶ ἠλέησέν σε.
bsb_english: But Jesus would not allow him. "Go home to your own people," He said, "and tell them how much the Lord has done for you, and what mercy He has shown you."

translation.thai: แต่พระองค์ไม่ทรงอนุญาต ตรัสกับเขาว่า 'จงกลับไปยังบ้านของเจ้า ไปหาคนของเจ้า และบอกเล่าให้พวกเขาฟังถึงสิ่งที่องค์พระผู้เป็นเจ้าได้ทรงกระทำเพื่อเจ้า และที่พระองค์ได้ทรงพระเมตตาต่อเจ้า'

key_decisions.greek: οὐκ ἀφῆκεν αὐτόν, ἀλλὰ λέγει (hist. pres.)
key_decisions.thai: ไม่ทรงอนุญาต ตรัสกับเขาว่า
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW grammar-connect-words-phrases: ἀλλά contrastive — Jesus DOES NOT permit (the request) BUT he DOES speak (this commission). Royal 'ทรงอนุญาต' + 'ตรัส' for Jesus. Historic present (per uW v.19) λέγει preserved as bare narrative present in Thai.

key_decisions.greek: Ὕπαγε εἰς τὸν οἶκόν σου πρὸς τοὺς σούς
key_decisions.thai: จงกลับไปยังบ้านของเจ้า ไปหาคนของเจ้า
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW figs-explicit on 'τοὺς σούς': could mean (1) family/relatives/friends or (2) family only. We render generically 'คนของเจ้า' (your people) preserving ambiguity. Jesus to the man uses 'เจ้า' (intimate-superior-to-subordinate, fits divine address to a healed-one — consistent with Mark 2:5 paralytic, 3:5 withered-hand-man per glossary precedent).

key_decisions.thai: บอกเล่าให้พวกเขาฟัง
key_decisions.rationale: ἀπαγγέλλω = 'announce, report.' Thai 'บอกเล่าให้...ฟัง' (tell-narrate-to-listen) preserves both the announcement function and the personal narrative quality. Note: this REVERSES the typical Markan secrecy command (cf. 1:44, 5:43 — keep silent!). The Gentile man is COMMANDED to publicize. Why? He's outside Jewish messianic-misunderstanding categories; in Decapolis the proclamation cannot fuel a wrong messianic expectation.

key_decisions.thai: องค์พระผู้เป็นเจ้า
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW figs-explicit: 'the Lord' could refer to (1) God or (2) Jesus himself. Thai per glossary: 'องค์พระผู้เป็นเจ้า' (standard for κύριος in YHWH-context). The ambiguity is theologically rich — the man will tell about Jesus (v.20: 'how much Jesus had done'), revealing that Jesus IS κύριος. Mark plants the equation. We preserve the formal Lord-title; the reader unpacks the identification at v.20.

key_decisions.thai: ได้ทรงกระทำเพื่อเจ้า...และที่พระองค์ได้ทรงพระเมตตาต่อเจ้า
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW figs-parallelism: 'has done for you' + 'had mercy on you' are not two separate events — one is interpretation of the other. Royal 'ได้ทรงกระทำ' (has-royal-done) + 'ได้ทรงพระเมตตา' (has-royal-mercied — the standard Thai royal verb for 'show mercy/compassion'). 'ทรงพระเมตตา' is the dignified Thai form, fitting the Lord-action. The two-clause parallel preserved — Thai naturally accommodates the doublet for emphasis.

translation.notes: REVERSE-SECRECY: Jesus normally commands silence (1:44, 7:36, 8:30) but commands publicization here. Why? The healed man is a Gentile in a Gentile region — no risk of fueling Jewish-political-messiah expectations. He's free to be the first Gentile evangelist. THE LORD = JESUS (v.20 makes the equation): the man tells how 'Jesus' did this, revealing that 'the Lord' (κύριος) WAS Jesus. Mark's high Christology peeks through. AT-HOME-MISSION: the man wanted to follow Jesus across the lake; Jesus sends him home as a witness. Discipleship can require staying, not going.
translation.thai_summary: ตามปกติพระเยซูทรงห้ามคนประกาศเรื่องของพระองค์ (1:44, 7:36, 8:30) แต่ที่นี่ทรงสั่งให้ประกาศ — เพราะเป็นดินแดนของคนต่างชาติ ไม่มีความเสี่ยงที่จะปลุกความคาดหวังเรื่องพระเมสสิยาห์ทางการเมืองแบบชาวยิว ชายคนนี้จึงได้เป็นผู้ประกาศข่าวประเสริฐคนแรกในหมู่คนต่างชาติ

greek: καὶ ἀπῆλθεν καὶ ἤρξατο κηρύσσειν ἐν τῇ Δεκαπόλει ὅσα ἐποίησεν αὐτῷ ὁ Ἰησοῦς, καὶ πάντες ἐθαύμαζον.
bsb_english: So the man went away and began to proclaim throughout the Decapolis how much Jesus had done for him. And everyone was amazed.

translation.thai: ชายนั้นก็ไปและเริ่มประกาศทั่วแคว้นทศบุรี ถึงสิ่งที่พระเยซูได้ทรงกระทำเพื่อเขา และทุกคนก็พากันอัศจรรย์ใจ

key_decisions.thai: เริ่มประกาศ
key_decisions.rationale: κηρύσσω = 'proclaim, herald, preach' — the same verb used for Jesus' own preaching (1:14, 1:38–39) and John the Baptist's (1:4, 1:7), and the apostles' commission (3:14, 6:12). Mark uses LOADED missional vocabulary for this Gentile man's testimony. Thai 'ประกาศ' (proclaim/announce) standard biblical term.

key_decisions.thai: ทั่วแคว้นทศบุรี
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW translate-names: Δεκάπολις = 'Ten Cities' — Greco-Roman federation east/southeast of Galilee (Scythopolis, Hippos, Gadara, Pella, Philadelphia, Gerasa, etc.). Thai 'ทศบุรี' (ten-city) is the standard Thai biblical transliteration-translation (Pali-derived 'ทศ' = ten + 'บุรี' = city). Some Thai Bibles use 'เดคาโปลิส' (transliteration) — we follow 'ทศบุรี' as the more established biblical-Thai form.

key_decisions.thai: สิ่งที่พระเยซูได้ทรงกระทำเพื่อเขา
key_decisions.rationale: ECHO + UPDATE of v.19: Jesus said 'tell what THE LORD did for you'; the man tells 'what JESUS did for him.' Mark identifies κύριος = Ἰησοῦς. Royal 'ได้ทรงกระทำ' parallels v.19 — preserving the verbal echo that makes the Christological equation. 'เพื่อเขา' (for him) for dative αὐτῷ.

key_decisions.thai: ทุกคนก็พากันอัศจรรย์ใจ
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW figs-nominaladj: 'all' = all hearers. θαυμάζω = wonder, marvel. Imperfect = ongoing astonishment as the testimony spread. Thai 'ทุกคนก็พากันอัศจรรย์ใจ' (everyone gathered to-marvel-heart) — 'พากัน' (collectively/in-company) preserves the social-spread; 'อัศจรรย์ใจ' (marvel-heart) standard Thai for amazement.

translation.notes: FIRST GENTILE EVANGELIST: a single sentence captures the entire Decapolis mission. CHRISTOLOGICAL EQUATION: Jesus said 'the Lord' (κύριος) — the man tells about 'Jesus' (Ἰησοῦς). For first readers, this is the equation in plain text: Jesus is the Lord. STRUCTURAL CLOSE: the Gerasene episode ends not with Jesus' departure but with the witness's mission. Mark's first major Gentile narrative concludes on a missional note that anticipates Acts 1:8.
translation.thai_summary: ความลงตัวทางคริสตวิทยา: พระเยซูตรัสว่า 'องค์พระผู้เป็นเจ้า' (ยาห์เวห์) ทรงทำสิ่งยิ่งใหญ่ ชายคนนั้นไปเล่าว่า 'พระเยซู' ทรงทำสิ่งยิ่งใหญ่ — ในประโยคเดียว มาระโกประกาศชัดเจนว่าพระเยซูคือองค์พระผู้เป็นเจ้า

greek: Καὶ διαπεράσαντος τοῦ Ἰησοῦ ἐν τῷ πλοίῳ πάλιν εἰς τὸ πέραν συνήχθη ὄχλος πολὺς ἐπ' αὐτόν, καὶ ἦν παρὰ τὴν θάλασσαν.
bsb_english: When Jesus had again crossed by boat to the other side, a large crowd gathered around Him beside the sea.

translation.thai: เมื่อพระเยซูเสด็จลงเรือข้ามทะเลกลับไปยังอีกฟากหนึ่งอีกครั้งหนึ่ง ฝูงชนเป็นอันมากก็มาชุมนุมล้อมรอบพระองค์ ขณะที่พระองค์ประทับอยู่ริมทะเล

key_decisions.greek: διαπεράσαντος τοῦ Ἰησοῦ ἐν τῷ πλοίῳ πάλιν εἰς τὸ πέραν (gen. abs.)
key_decisions.thai: เมื่อพระเยซูเสด็จลงเรือข้ามทะเลกลับไปยังอีกฟากหนึ่งอีกครั้งหนึ่ง
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW writing-newevent + figs-synecdoche: new major event; Jesus represents Jesus + disciples. Thai 'เสด็จลงเรือข้าม' (royal-embark-cross) preserves royal action; 'กลับไปยังอีกฟากหนึ่ง' (return-to-other-side) — RETURN to the western (Jewish) shore, reversing the crossing of v.1. 'ปาλιν' (per uW figs-explicit v.21): refers to the prior crossing, now reversed.

key_decisions.thai: ฝูงชนเป็นอันมากก็มาชุมนุมล้อมรอบ
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW figs-activepassive: passive 'was gathered.' Thai 'ฝูงชนเป็นอันมาก' (large crowd) consistent with prior chapters; 'มาชุมนุมล้อมรอบ' parallel to Mark 4:1's gathering. Same Mark 3:7–4:1 pattern: Jesus arrives, crowds press around.

key_decisions.thai: ขณะที่พระองค์ประทับอยู่ริมทะเล
key_decisions.rationale: Imperfect = ongoing/setting verb. παρά + acc. 'beside.' Thai 'ขณะที่...ประทับอยู่ริมทะเล' (while...royal-stay-by-shore-of-sea) preserves the scene-setting feel. Royal 'ประทับ' for Jesus' located posture. Mirrors Mark 4:1 ('παρὰ τὴν θάλασσαν') — Jesus returns to his lakeside teaching post.

translation.notes: TRANSITION: closes the Gerasene cycle, reopens Galilean ministry. Geographically: back on the Jewish (western) shore. Mark's sandwich begins here — vv.21–24 set up Jairus, vv.25–34 interrupt with the woman, vv.35–43 resume Jairus. The intercalation (Markan sandwich) is one of his signature literary devices.

greek: καὶ ἔρχεται εἷς τῶν ἀρχισυναγώγων, ὀνόματι Ἰάϊρος, καὶ ἰδὼν αὐτὸν πίπτει πρὸς τοὺς πόδας αὐτοῦ
bsb_english: A synagogue leader named Jairus arrived, and seeing Jesus, he fell at His feet

translation.thai: ดูเถิด มีนายธรรมศาลาคนหนึ่งชื่อไยรัส มาหาพระองค์ เมื่อเห็นพระองค์ก็หมอบกราบลงแทบพระบาท

key_decisions.thai: มาหา...ก็หมอบกราบลง
key_decisions.rationale: Two historic presents (per uW v.22) — vivid pacing for the new episode. Thai uses simple narrative verbs.

key_decisions.thai: นายธรรมศาลาคนหนึ่งชื่อไยรัส
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW writing-participants + translate-names: introducing Jairus as new character. ἀρχισυνάγωγος = synagogue-ruler (lay-leader responsible for the worship service in a local synagogue — significant social standing). Thai 'นายธรรมศาลา' (chief-of-synagogue) standard biblical term; 'ธรรมศาลา' per glossary. 'ชื่อไยรัส' (named Jairus) — Hebrew יָאִיר meaning 'he enlightens.' Standard Thai transliteration 'ไยรัส.'

key_decisions.thai: ดูเถิด
key_decisions.rationale: Some MS witnesses include ἰδού here (cf. uW v.22 note); SBLGNT does not but the construction 'καὶ ἔρχεται εἷς...' carries the same attention-getting force. Thai opens with 'ดูเถิด' (Behold!) preserving the narrative-attention call. (If pure SBLGNT-following is preferred, this 'ดูเถิด' could be dropped — but Thai narration accommodates it as discourse marker for new-event.)

key_decisions.thai: หมอบกราบลงแทบพระบาท
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW translate-symaction: falling at feet = posture of profound respect/supplication. Thai 'หมอบกราบลงแทบพระบาท' (prostrate-kneel-down-near-royal-feet) preserves both the physical action and the royal register — 'แทบพระบาท' (at the royal feet) is the dignified Thai expression for falling before a king/divine being. Jairus, though socially elevated, lowers himself entirely before Jesus. This sets up the trust-and-faith theme of the chapter.

translation.notes: JAIRUS = lay-elder, head of a local synagogue. His public submission to Jesus (a non-establishment teacher) is socially costly — synagogue authorities are typically presented as antagonists in Mark (cf. 1:21–28, 3:1–6). Yet Mark records this exception: a synagogue ruler's daughter is dying, and he recognizes Jesus' authority over death. The Hebrew name יָאִיר ('he enlightens') may be a Markan irony — the synagogue official seeks light from the One the synagogue establishment opposes.

greek: καὶ παρακαλεῖ αὐτὸν πολλὰ λέγων ὅτι Τὸ θυγάτριόν μου ἐσχάτως ἔχει, ἵνα ἐλθὼν ἐπιθῇς τὰς χεῖρας αὐτῇ ἵνα σωθῇ καὶ ζήσῃ.
bsb_english: and pleaded with Him urgently, "My little daughter is near death. Please come and place Your hands on her, so that she will be healed and live."

translation.thai: และทูลวิงวอนพระองค์อย่างยิ่งว่า 'ลูกสาวเล็กๆ ของข้าพระองค์ใกล้จะสิ้นใจแล้ว ขอเชิญเสด็จไปวางพระหัตถ์บนตัวเธอ เพื่อเธอจะหายและรอดชีวิต'

key_decisions.thai: ทูลวิงวอนพระองค์อย่างยิ่งว่า
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW v.23 historic present. 'ทูล' (deferential speech-verb) + 'วิงวอน' (entreat) preserves the urgent supplication; 'อย่างยิ่ง' (very much) for adverbial πολλά. Jairus uses humble register throughout per glossary precedent (Mark 1:40 leper).

key_decisions.thai: ลูกสาวเล็กๆ ของข้าพระองค์
key_decisions.rationale: θυγάτριον = diminutive of θυγάτηρ — 'little daughter' (affectionate; she's twelve per v.42). Thai 'ลูกสาวเล็กๆ' (daughter-little-little — reduplication for diminutive force) preserves both the size-diminutive and the affectionate tone. 'ข้าพระองค์' (humble-1st-person to royal-addressee) per Mark 1:40 glossary precedent.

key_decisions.thai: ใกล้จะสิ้นใจแล้ว
key_decisions.rationale: HAPAX LEGOMENON: ἐσχάτως (adv.) only here in NT. Per uW figs-euphemism: idiom 'is at the last' = is dying / on her deathbed. Cognate with ἔσχατος ('last, end'). Thai 'ใกล้จะสิ้นใจ' (near to-exhale-the-last-breath — Thai poetic euphemism for dying); 'แล้ว' (perfective particle) marks the imminence. 'สิ้นใจ' is dignified Thai for death — appropriate for a parent's anguished speech, neither bluntly clinical nor sentimentalized.

key_decisions.greek: ἵνα ἐλθὼν ἐπιθῇς τὰς χεῖρας αὐτῇ ἵνα σωθῇ καὶ ζήσῃ
key_decisions.thai: ขอเชิญเสด็จไปวางพระหัตถ์บนตัวเธอ เพื่อเธอจะหายและรอดชีวิต
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW grammar-connect-logic-result: ἵνα introduces what Jairus wants Jesus to do. 'ขอเชิญเสด็จ' (please-invite royal-go) preserves humble request + royal action. 'พระหัตถ์' (royal-hand) per RULES.md §3. 'หาย' (be cured) + 'รอดชีวิต' (survive/be saved-alive) preserves the σῴζω + ζάω doublet — both healing AND continuing life. Per uW figs-activepassive: σωθῇ ('be saved') passive — Jesus is implicit agent; Thai active 'หาย' acceptable. The doubled hope ('healed AND live') signals the gravity: she's not just sick, she's dying.

translation.notes: HAPAX: ἐσχάτως (5:23 only). The double purpose-clause (σωθῇ καὶ ζήσῃ) is a father's anguished hope: 'that she be healed AND live' — both emphases needed because she is dying. SOCIAL DYNAMIC: a synagogue ruler kneeling and pleading publicly with Jesus. The father has set aside his religious-political position because his daughter's life is at stake.

greek: καὶ ἀπῆλθεν μετ' αὐτοῦ. Καὶ ἠκολούθει αὐτῷ ὄχλος πολύς, καὶ συνέθλιβον αὐτόν.
bsb_english: So Jesus went with him, and a large crowd followed and pressed around Him.

translation.thai: พระองค์ก็เสด็จไปกับเขา และฝูงชนเป็นอันมากก็ตามเสด็จไป เบียดเสียดล้อมรอบพระองค์

key_decisions.thai: เสด็จไปกับเขา
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW figs-synecdoche: Jesus + disciples. Royal 'เสด็จไป' (royal-depart) for Jesus' movement; 'กับเขา' (with him — Jairus). Disciples implicit per Mark 5:37.

key_decisions.thai: ฝูงชนเป็นอันมากก็ตามเสด็จไป
key_decisions.rationale: Imperfect = ongoing following. Thai 'ตามเสด็จ' (follow-royal-go — the royal verb for following royalty) preserves the dignity-of-following. Note: Mark distinguishes ἀκολουθέω (follow) from the disciples' specific call-following — here it's the curious crowd, not committed disciples.

key_decisions.thai: เบียดเสียดล้อมรอบ
key_decisions.rationale: συνθλίβω = 'press together, throng.' Imperfect = sustained pressing. Thai 'เบียดเสียด' (jostle-press) idiomatic for crowded human bodies pressing against each other; 'ล้อมรอบ' (surround) preserves the spatial encirclement. This sets up v.27 — the woman approaches IN the press.

translation.notes: SETUP for the intercalation: the dense crowd is the cover under which the hemorrhaging woman approaches. The press provides the condition for her to touch Jesus' garment unnoticed.

greek: καὶ γυνὴ οὖσα ἐν ῥύσει αἵματος δώδεκα ἔτη
bsb_english: And a woman was there who had suffered from bleeding for twelve years.

translation.thai: มีหญิงคนหนึ่ง ผู้ป่วยด้วยโรคโลหิตตกมาเป็นเวลาสิบสองปี

key_decisions.thai: มีหญิงคนหนึ่ง
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW writing-participants: introducing the woman as new character. Mark uses a long participial chain (vv.25–27) before the main verb ἥψατο in v.27. Thai breaks this into separate clauses for natural reading. 'มีหญิงคนหนึ่ง' (there-was a-woman a-certain-one) introduces.

key_decisions.thai: ผู้ป่วยด้วยโรคโลหิตตก
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW figs-euphemism: 'being in a flow of blood' = euphemism for chronic abnormal menstrual bleeding. Thai 'โรคโลหิตตก' (disease blood-fall — the standard Thai biblical/medical term for hemorrhage, especially gynecological) preserves the dignified medical-euphemistic register. 'ผู้ป่วยด้วย' (sufferer-from) preserves οὖσα ἐν as a condition-state. RITUAL IMPURITY: under Lev 15:25–30, a woman with ongoing flow of blood is in continuous niddah-impurity, untouchable, isolated socially and religiously. This explains both her secrecy (v.27) and her later fear (v.33).

key_decisions.thai: มาเป็นเวลาสิบสองปี
key_decisions.rationale: 12 years — same number as the girl's age in v.42 (δώδεκα ἐτῶν). DELIBERATE NUMERICAL ECHO: Mark sets the woman's affliction-duration = the girl's lifespan. The intercalation is bound by this 12. Thai 'สิบสองปี' standard.

translation.notes: RITUAL IMPURITY: chronic menstrual hemorrhage under Lev 15:25–30 = continuous niddah-state, perpetual ritual uncleanness, social/religious isolation, anyone she touches becomes unclean. NUMERICAL ECHO: 12 years of flow / 12-year-old girl — the woman has been suffering for the entire span of the girl's life. Mark binds the two stories not just by sandwiching but by this numerical resonance: two women defined by 12, both restored by one touch.
translation.thai_summary: ในกฎของโมเสส (เลวีนิติ 15:25-30) หญิงที่มีโลหิตไหลต่อเนื่องจะอยู่ในสภาพไม่สะอาดตามพิธีตลอดเวลา ต้องแยกตัวจากสังคมและวัด ใครแตะตัวเธอหรือของที่เธอแตะก็จะกลายเป็นคนไม่สะอาด 12 ปีของนางเท่ากับอายุของเด็กหญิงที่พระเยซูทรงไปช่วยในตอนต่อไป

greek: καὶ πολλὰ παθοῦσα ὑπὸ πολλῶν ἰατρῶν καὶ δαπανήσασα τὰ παρ' αὐτῆς πάντα καὶ μηδὲν ὠφεληθεῖσα ἀλλὰ μᾶλλον εἰς τὸ χεῖρον ἐλθοῦσα,
bsb_english: She had borne much agony under the care of many physicians and had spent all she had, but to no avail. Instead, her condition had only grown worse.

translation.thai: นางได้ทนทุกข์ทรมานมามากจากการรักษาของหมอหลายคน เสียทรัพย์สินทั้งหมดของนางจนสิ้นเนื้อประดาตัว แต่ไม่ได้ดีขึ้นเลย กลับเลวร้ายลงเสียอีก

key_decisions.thai: ได้ทนทุกข์ทรมานมามากจากการรักษาของหมอหลายคน
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW figs-explicit (twofold): suffered FROM the treatments OR suffered WHILE being treated. Mark's Greek is ambiguous; we render as 'จากการรักษา' (from-the-treatment) which leans toward the treatments themselves causing suffering — historically plausible (1st-century medicine often used emetics, purgatives, leech-therapy, magical formulas). Thai 'ทนทุกข์ทรมาน' (endure-suffering-torture) standard biblical phrasing for severe suffering.

key_decisions.thai: เสียทรัพย์สินทั้งหมดของนางจนสิ้นเนื้อประดาตัว
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW figs-explicit + figs-idiom: 'spent everything from herself' = spent all she had on physicians' fees. Thai 'เสียทรัพย์สิน' (spend-property) + 'จนสิ้นเนื้อประดาตัว' (until-bare-flesh-of-body — the standard Thai idiom for 'down to nothing,' 'destitute'). Captures both the financial ruin and the desperate trajectory.

key_decisions.thai: แต่ไม่ได้ดีขึ้นเลย
key_decisions.rationale: ὠφελέω (pass.) = 'be benefited.' Thai 'ไม่ได้ดีขึ้นเลย' (not-become-better at-all) natural rendering. The rebuttal of investment: nothing gained.

key_decisions.thai: กลับเลวร้ายลงเสียอีก
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW figs-idiom: 'having come to the worse' = getting worse. Thai 'กลับเลวร้ายลงเสียอีก' (instead-bad-down also-more) — 'กลับ' (instead) preserves the frustrating reversal.

translation.notes: Background-information stack (per uW v.26 writing-background): three things are true of this woman before her decisive touch — much suffering, complete financial ruin, worsening condition. The pile-up of perfect-aspect participles (παθοῦσα, δαπανήσασα, ὠφεληθεῖσα, ἐλθοῦσα) intensifies the hopelessness against which the touch will be all-or-nothing. SUBTLE COMMENTARY on 1st-century medicine: physicians made things worse, not better.

greek: ἀκούσασα περὶ τοῦ Ἰησοῦ, ἐλθοῦσα ἐν τῷ ὄχλῳ ὄπισθεν ἥψατο τοῦ ἱματίου αὐτοῦ·
bsb_english: When the woman heard about Jesus, she came up through the crowd behind Him and touched His cloak.

translation.thai: เมื่อนางได้ยินเรื่องของพระเยซู ก็แทรกตัวเข้าไปในฝูงชนทางด้านหลัง และแตะต้องฉลองพระองค์

key_decisions.thai: เมื่อนางได้ยินเรื่องของพระเยซู
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW figs-explicit: she had heard about Jesus' healings. Thai 'ได้ยินเรื่อง' (heard-stories) preserves the rumor-circulation — she'd heard, presumably, that Jesus heals.

key_decisions.thai: ก็แทรกตัวเข้าไปในฝูงชนทางด้านหลัง
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW figs-go: 'gone' from her perspective. Thai 'แทรกตัวเข้าไป' (insert-self-go-in) makes the crowd-pushing explicit, capturing the deliberate effort it took an unclean woman to enter a crowd. 'ทางด้านหลัง' (from-the-rear) for ὄπισθεν — secrecy is critical (her impurity is contagious; if known, she'd be expelled).

key_decisions.thai: แตะต้องฉลองพระองค์
key_decisions.rationale: ἅπτω (mid.) = touch. ἱμάτιον = outer garment, cloak. Per uW figs-extrainfo: don't explain why here — Mark gives reason in v.28. Thai 'แตะต้อง' (touch-make-contact) — the doublet preserves the deliberate contact-making, more than accidental brushing. 'ฉลองพระองค์' is the dignified royal Thai for 'royal garment' — preserves the sacred quality (Jesus' garment, not just a cloak). The woman's touch is forbidden under Lev 15:27 (whoever touches anything she touches becomes unclean) — yet she reverses it. Possibly evokes the Numbers 15:38–39 fringe (tzitzit) on a Jewish man's outer garment — the Matt 9:20 par. specifies κρασπέδου (fringe). Mark says only ἱμάτιον; we follow Mark.

translation.notes: Per uW figs-extrainfo: the verse withholds the reason for the touch — v.28 supplies it. RITUAL VIOLATION: Lev 15:27 — touching her or what she touches transmits impurity. She knowingly violates the purity code, but Mark frames her act as faith-driven. The cloak-touch evokes the Numbers 15:38 tzitzit (fringes on the corner of a Jewish man's outer garment) believed by some 1st-century Jews to carry blessing/healing power — Matt 9:20 parallel makes this explicit (κρασπέδου = fringe); Mark says only ἱμάτιον.
translation.thai_summary: การแตะชายฉลองพระองค์ของพระเยซูอาจเป็นการแตะ 'ซิซิท' พู่ตามกฎของโมเสสในกันดารวิถี 15:38-39 มาลาคี 4:2 พยากรณ์ว่า 'ดวงอาทิตย์แห่งความชอบธรรมจะขึ้น มีปีก' (คำเดียวกับ 'ชายฉลองพระองค์') นางอาจเข้าใจว่าพระเยซูคือผู้ที่มาถึงในคำพยากรณ์นั้น

greek: ἔλεγεν γὰρ ὅτι Ἐὰν ἅψωμαι κἂν τῶν ἱματίων αὐτοῦ σωθήσομαι.
bsb_english: For she kept saying, "If only I touch His garments, I will be healed."

translation.thai: เพราะนางคิดอยู่ในใจว่า 'ขอเพียงข้าได้แตะแม้แต่ฉลองพระองค์ของท่าน ข้าก็จะหาย'

key_decisions.thai: เพราะนางคิดอยู่ในใจ
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW grammar-connect-logic-result + figs-explicit: γάρ explains v.27. Imperfect ἔλεγεν = repeated/sustained inner speech. Per uW figs-explicit: she was 'saying' to herself — i.e., thinking. Thai 'คิดอยู่ในใจ' (think-staying-in-heart) makes the inner-speech aspect explicit.

key_decisions.greek: Ἐὰν ἅψωμαι κἂν τῶν ἱματίων αὐτοῦ
key_decisions.thai: ขอเพียงข้าได้แตะแม้แต่ฉลองพระองค์ของท่าน
key_decisions.rationale: ἐάν + aor. subj. = third-class condition (uncertain hope). κἄν = 'even if (only).' Per uW figs-explicit: 'just' indicates minimum action — she thinks even minimal contact will suffice. Thai 'ขอเพียง...แม้แต่' (please-only...even) preserves the modesty of expectation. Note the woman, in her INNER speech, refers to Jesus as 'ท่าน' (respectful but not yet the divine 'พระองค์') — her faith is still tentative; she doesn't yet know him as Lord. 'ข้า' (humble-1st-person, but not yet the most-humble 'ข้าพระองค์') — appropriate for inner-speech of someone who has not yet personally addressed Jesus.

key_decisions.thai: ก็จะหาย
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW figs-activepassive: 'I will be saved/healed.' σῴζω carries both physical healing and spiritual salvation senses — Mark exploits the double meaning. Here Thai 'หาย' (cured) for the immediate physical healing meaning; the spiritual-salvation overtone surfaces in v.34 where Jesus interprets her σῴζω.

translation.notes: Per uW translate-versebridge: vv.27–28 could be combined to put motivation before action; we keep Mark's order (action then motivation) which preserves his suspense-building. PSYCHOLOGY: Mark gives us interior monologue — rare in his fast narrative. The woman's faith is tentative but bold: she expects healing from minimal physical contact. This will become the basis of Jesus' 'your faith has saved you' in v.34.

greek: καὶ εὐθὺς ἐξηράνθη ἡ πηγὴ τοῦ αἵματος αὐτῆς, καὶ ἔγνω τῷ σώματι ὅτι ἴαται ἀπὸ τῆς μάστιγος.
bsb_english: Immediately her bleeding stopped, and she sensed in her body that she was healed of her affliction.

translation.thai: ในทันใดนั้น โลหิตที่ไหลของนางก็หยุด และนางก็รู้สึกได้ในร่างกายว่าตนหายจากโรคแล้ว

key_decisions.thai: ในทันใดนั้น โลหิตที่ไหล...ก็หยุด
key_decisions.rationale: Mark's signature εὐθύς. Per uW figs-activepassive: ξηραίνω (pass.) 'be dried up.' Thai 'หยุด' (stop) for the cessation; literal 'dry up' would be misleading for a flow-of-blood. Note ξηραίνω is the SAME verb as Mark 3:1 (withered hand) and Mark 4:6 (scorched seedlings) — Mark's lexical thread of organic 'drying'. 'ในทันใดนั้น' (in-that-instant) for εὐθύς.

key_decisions.thai: โลหิตที่ไหลของนาง
key_decisions.rationale: πηγή = 'spring, fountain, source.' Greek frames the chronic bleeding as a 'fountain of her blood' — the LXX uses πηγὴ τοῦ αἵματος in Lev 12:7 specifically for the post-childbirth bleeding-source. Mark may evoke the LXX phrasing. Thai compresses 'fountain of her blood' to 'โลหิตที่ไหลของนาง' (the-blood-that-flows of-her) — preserves the chronic-flow sense without an image-shift; Thai readers don't have the same Levitical-spring imagery.

key_decisions.thai: รู้สึกได้ในร่างกาย
key_decisions.rationale: γινώσκω = know. Dative of means τῷ σώματι = 'by/in her body.' Thai 'รู้สึกได้ในร่างกาย' (sense/feel-in-body) preserves the embodied epistemology — she didn't just hope she was healed; she felt the change. This sets up the body-knowledge parallel with Jesus' body-knowledge in v.30.

key_decisions.thai: ตนหายจากโรคแล้ว
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW figs-activepassive + figs-abstractnouns: 'has been healed from the affliction.' ἰάομαι (perf. pass.) = 'has been healed' (abiding state). μάστιξ literally = 'whip, scourge'; metaphorically = 'severe affliction' (esp. chronic disease — cf. NT only Mark 3:10, 5:29, 5:34, Luke 7:21, Acts 22:24). Thai 'หายจากโรค' (be-cured of-disease) — 'หาย' standard biblical for medical recovery; 'แล้ว' marks the perfect-aspect already-accomplished sense. The 'whip/scourge' metaphor doesn't transfer naturally — Thai readers would hear it as either literal whip or punishment, neither of which fits. We render to 'โรค' (disease) for clarity.

translation.notes: LEXICAL ECHO: ξηραίνω (dried up) — Mark's signature verb. Used at 3:1 (withered hand), 4:6 (scorched seedlings), and now 5:29 (dried-up bleeding). Three different things 'dry up' in Mark but the Greek lexeme unifies them. Thai must vary the verb (Mark 3 'ลีบ' for atrophy; Mark 4 'เหี่ยวแห้ง' for plant-wilt; Mark 5 'หยุด' for blood-cessation) — the unity of the Greek verb is lost but each Thai rendering is the natural one for its context. μάστιξ ('scourge') as metaphor for chronic disease — Mark's distinctive vocabulary; ancient readers might also have heard the implication of divine chastisement (cf. Job 21:9 LXX μάστιξ θεοῦ).

greek: καὶ εὐθὺς ὁ Ἰησοῦς ἐπιγνοὺς ἐν ἑαυτῷ τὴν ἐξ αὐτοῦ δύναμιν ἐξελθοῦσαν ἐπιστραφεὶς ἐν τῷ ὄχλῳ ἔλεγεν· Τίς μου ἥψατο τῶν ἱματίων;
bsb_english: At once Jesus was aware that power had gone out from Him. Turning to the crowd, He asked, "Who touched My garments?"

translation.thai: ในทันใดนั้น พระเยซูทรงทราบในพระองค์เองว่ามีฤทธิ์อำนาจออกไปจากพระองค์ จึงทรงหันมาในฝูงชนและตรัสว่า 'ใครแตะต้องฉลองพระองค์ของเรา?'

key_decisions.thai: ทรงทราบในพระองค์เอง
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW figs-explicit: Jesus knew internally — through inward perception, not external observation. ἐπιγινώσκω 'recognize, perceive fully.' Royal 'ทรงทราบ' (royal-know) for divine cognition; 'ในพระองค์เอง' (within-himself) preserves the introspective ἐν ἑαυτῷ. This parallels the woman's body-knowledge (ἔγνω τῷ σώματι v.29): she knew through her body that she was healed; he knew through his body that power had gone out. Mark builds a corporeal-cognition pair.

key_decisions.thai: มีฤทธิ์อำนาจออกไปจากพระองค์
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW figs-explicit + figs-abstractnouns: δύναμις = power (here the healing power, considered as something that can flow). Thai 'ฤทธิ์อำนาจ' (might-authority — paired Thai noun standard for divine power) preserves the abstract noun while pairing 'ฤทธิ์' (might/miraculous-power) and 'อำนาจ' (authority) — the Thai biblical idiom. 'ออกไปจากพระองค์' (go-out from him) preserves the spatial-egression metaphor. This does not imply Jesus was depleted (per uW v.30 explicit note); it's simply that he sensed the outflow.

key_decisions.thai: จึงทรงหันมาในฝูงชน
key_decisions.rationale: ἐπιστρέφομαι = turn around. Royal 'ทรงหันมา' (royal-turn). 'ในฝูงชน' (in/within the-crowd) preserves the spatial dynamic — Jesus turns within the press, not away from it.

key_decisions.thai: ใครแตะต้องฉลองพระองค์ของเรา?
key_decisions.rationale: Direct question. Royal Jesus self-reference 'เรา' per glossary (Mark 3:33 precedent). 'ฉลองพระองค์ของเรา' (royal-garment of-us) preserves the dignified register. Note: Jesus does not yet say 'who has been healed?' — he asks who touched, deferring identification of the act's healing-implication. The question creates the public exposure the woman feared.

translation.notes: BODY-KNOWLEDGE PAIR: she knew in her body she was healed (v.29); he knew in himself that power had gone out (v.30). Mark portrays a kind of dialogical embodied awareness — her faith and his power interacting at a bodily level. THEOLOGICAL CRUX: did Jesus 'know who' touched him? Mark's text suggests he knew SOMETHING happened but had to ask to bring it into the open. This is consistent with Mark's portrayal of Jesus as both fully divine and incarnationally limited (cf. Mark 13:32). Other interpreters argue he knew but asked publicly to draw out the woman's confession.

greek: καὶ ἔλεγον αὐτῷ οἱ μαθηταὶ αὐτοῦ· Βλέπεις τὸν ὄχλον συνθλίβοντά σε, καὶ λέγεις· Τίς μου ἥψατο;
bsb_english: His disciples answered, "You can see the crowd pressing in on You, and yet You ask, 'Who touched Me?'"

translation.thai: เหล่าสาวกของพระองค์ทูลว่า 'พระองค์ทอดพระเนตรเห็นฝูงชนเบียดเสียดพระองค์อยู่ แล้วพระองค์ยังตรัสถามว่า "ใครแตะต้องเรา?" หรือ?'

key_decisions.thai: พระองค์ทอดพระเนตรเห็นฝูงชนเบียดเสียดพระองค์อยู่
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW figs-explicit: implies anyone could have touched. Royal 'ทอดพระเนตรเห็น' (royal-see) per RULES.md §3 — disciples address Jesus with full ราชาศัพท์. 'เบียดเสียด' (jostle-press) consistent with v.24.

key_decisions.thai: แล้วพระองค์ยังตรัสถามว่า...หรือ?
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW figs-rquestion + grammar-connect-words-phrases (καί contrastive 'and yet'): the disciples find Jesus' question absurd. Thai 'แล้วพระองค์ยัง...หรือ?' (and royal-yet...question?) preserves the incredulous rhetorical-question form. 'ยัง' (still/yet) carries the 'and you STILL ask' force.

key_decisions.thai: ใครแตะต้องเรา?
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW figs-quotesinquotes: quote within quote. Thai uses double quotes nested within single quotes for the disciples' quotation of Jesus' words. Note Jesus' self-reference 'เรา' is preserved per his royal-self register, even within the disciples' quotation.

key_decisions.thai: ยังตรัสถาม
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW figs-irony: the disciples mock the question. Thai 'ยังตรัสถาม' preserves the verbal echo with light incredulity — 'ยัง' carries the 'STILL asking' impatience without ridiculing Jesus.

translation.notes: DISCIPLE INCOMPREHENSION: the disciples see physical contact but can't sense the difference between casual jostling and faith-charged touch. Mark uses the disciples as foils for the woman's faith — she perceived what they couldn't. Recurring Markan theme (cf. 4:13, 6:52, 7:18, 8:17–21).

greek: καὶ περιεβλέπετο ἰδεῖν τὴν τοῦτο ποιήσασαν.
bsb_english: But He kept looking around to see who had done this.

translation.thai: แต่พระองค์ก็ยังคงทอดพระเนตรไปรอบๆ เพื่อให้ทรงเห็นผู้ที่ได้กระทำการนี้

key_decisions.thai: แต่
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW grammar-connect-words-phrases: καί contrastive — Jesus disregards the disciples' dismissive response. Thai 'แต่' (but) preserves the contrast.

key_decisions.thai: ยังคงทอดพระเนตรไปรอบๆ
key_decisions.rationale: περιβλέπομαι = look around (middle voice — looking-for-oneself). Imperfect = sustained looking. Royal 'ทอดพระเนตร' (royal-see/look) per RULES.md §3. 'ยังคง...ไปรอบๆ' (still...around) preserves the persistent searching gaze.

key_decisions.thai: ผู้ที่ได้กระทำการนี้
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW writing-pronouns + writing-participants: 'the (woman) having done this.' FEMININE participle reveals to the reader (but not yet the disciples!) that the toucher is female. Thai 'ผู้' (one who) is gender-neutral — but Greek's fem. is part of Mark's narrative reveal-strategy. We could mark it explicitly ('หญิงผู้ที่...') but that would spoil the reveal pacing. We follow Greek's economy: Jesus knows it's a woman; the disciples are about to find out.

translation.notes: GENDER REVEAL: the feminine participle τὴν...ποιήσασαν tells the reader (and signals Jesus' insight) that the toucher is a woman — before the disciples know. Mark uses grammatical gender as a narrative device. Thai's gender-neutral 'ผู้' loses this; the reveal lands fully at v.33.

greek: ἡ δὲ γυνὴ φοβηθεῖσα καὶ τρέμουσα, εἰδυῖα ὃ γέγονεν αὐτῇ, ἦλθεν καὶ προσέπεσεν αὐτῷ καὶ εἶπεν αὐτῷ πᾶσαν τὴν ἀλήθειαν.
bsb_english: Then the woman, knowing what had happened to her, came and fell down before Him trembling in fear, and she told Him the whole truth.

translation.thai: ฝ่ายหญิงคนนั้น รู้ตัวว่าเกิดอะไรขึ้นกับนาง ก็ตัวสั่นด้วยความกลัว เข้ามาหมอบกราบลงเฉพาะพระพักตร์ และทูลความจริงทั้งหมดแด่พระองค์

key_decisions.thai: ตัวสั่นด้วยความกลัว
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW figs-hendiadys: 'fearing and trembling' = trembling with fear (one idea, two terms). Thai 'ตัวสั่นด้วยความกลัว' (body-trembles with fear) compresses the hendiadys into the natural Thai idiom. Why fear? She's exposed: ritually unclean woman who touched a holy teacher; fear of public shame, of Jesus' rebuke, of being expelled from the crowd.

key_decisions.thai: รู้ตัวว่าเกิดอะไรขึ้นกับนาง
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW grammar-connect-logic-result: gives reason for her behavior — she knew the healing had occurred AND knew her contact had been detected. Thai 'รู้ตัวว่าเกิดอะไรขึ้นกับนาง' (know-self that-happened with-her) — 'รู้ตัว' captures the self-aware-realization (she knows about herself, she knows about her healing).

key_decisions.thai: เข้ามาหมอบกราบลงเฉพาะพระพักตร์
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW translate-symaction: προσπίπτω = fall before — posture of worship/honor/submission. Thai 'หมอบกราบลงเฉพาะพระพักตร์' (prostrate-kneel-down at-royal-presence) preserves the posture and royal register — same construction as v.6 (the demoniac) but with very different inner content: the demoniac's prostration was forced; hers is freely-given confession.

key_decisions.thai: ทูลความจริงทั้งหมดแด่พระองค์
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW figs-explicit + figs-abstractnouns: 'the whole truth' = everything about her condition AND her surreptitious touch. Thai 'ทูลความจริงทั้งหมด' (deferential-tell the-truth-all) — 'ทูล' preserves the reverent speech-verb (humble-to-superior). 'แด่พระองค์' (to royal-him) full ราชาศัพท์ — once she steps forward in faith, her register elevates from inner-speech 'ท่าน' (v.28) to public 'พระองค์.'

translation.notes: REGISTER SHIFT: the woman's inner-speech in v.28 used 'ข้า/ท่าน' (humble-1st but tentative-respectful 2nd). Now in v.33 her direct address would use 'ข้าพระองค์/พระองค์' (full humble-to-divine register, per glossary). Thai narrator-voice 'แด่พระองค์' marks the shift to full reverence. Faith confessed publicly elevates her speech. PUBLIC CONFESSION: Mark insists on the woman's public testimony — Jesus could have noted the healing privately. The 'whole truth' becomes the doorway to v.34's commendation. Faith hidden gets healing; faith confessed gets benediction.

greek: ὁ δὲ εἶπεν αὐτῇ· Θυγάτηρ, ἡ πίστις σου σέσωκέν σε· ὕπαγε εἰς εἰρήνην, καὶ ἴσθι ὑγιὴς ἀπὸ τῆς μάστιγός σου.
bsb_english: "Daughter," said Jesus, "your faith has healed you. Go in peace and be free of your affliction."

translation.thai: พระองค์ตรัสกับนางว่า 'ลูกหญิงเอ๋ย ความเชื่อของเจ้าได้ทำให้เจ้ารอดแล้ว จงไปเป็นสุขเถิด และจงหายจากโรคของเจ้า'

key_decisions.thai: ลูกหญิงเอ๋ย
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW figs-metaphor: 'Daughter' = affectionate address from elder to younger; not a literal kinship claim. Thai 'ลูกหญิงเอ๋ย' (daughter-female-vocative-particle) — 'ลูกหญิง' is the gender-marked daughter; 'เอ๋ย' is the Thai vocative-affectionate particle (used in older biblical Thai to address with tenderness). Captures the warmth Jesus extends. Note: this is the ONLY place in the Gospels where Jesus addresses a woman as 'daughter.' The reciprocity is intimate — she's anonymous, ritually unclean, socially invisible; he names her into family.

key_decisions.thai: ความเชื่อของเจ้าได้ทำให้เจ้ารอดแล้ว
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW figs-abstractnouns + figs-personification: faith personified as the agent of salvation. πίστις = trust/faith (per glossary should be tracked across chapters; this is its first major Mark appearance). σῴζω here in PERFECT = 'has saved' (abiding state). Thai 'ความเชื่อของเจ้า' (your-faith) — 'ความเชื่อ' standard biblical Thai. 'ได้ทำให้เจ้ารอดแล้ว' (has-caused-you-to-be-saved-already) — perfect aspect with 'แล้ว.' σῴζω here is intentionally polysemous: physical healing + spiritual salvation. Thai 'รอด' (be saved/escape) carries both senses naturally — same word for being-rescued from danger AND being-saved spiritually.

key_decisions.thai: จงไปเป็นสุขเถิด
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW figs-idiom + figs-abstractnouns: Hebraism (lekh leshalom — 1 Sam 1:17, 20:42; 2 Sam 15:9). Standard Hebrew/Aramaic farewell-blessing. Thai 'จงไปเป็นสุขเถิด' (go-be-happy/peaceful) preserves the imperative + state-of-peace. 'เป็นสุข' (be-happy/well) standard Thai for εἰρήνη in well-wish context — broader than narrow 'peace' (สันติสุข), encompassing the Hebrew shalom's wholeness/welfare meaning.

key_decisions.greek: ἴσθι ὑγιὴς ἀπὸ τῆς μάστιγός σου
key_decisions.thai: จงหายจากโรคของเจ้า
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW figs-activepassive + figs-abstractnouns: 'be healthy from your affliction.' Imperative ἴσθι + adj. ὑγιής (healthy). Thai 'จงหายจากโรคของเจ้า' (be-cured of-your-disease) — performative imperative confirms the healing already happened, declaring its permanence. μάστιξ → 'โรค' consistent with v.29.

key_decisions.thai: เจ้า (intimate-affectionate, paternal-like)
key_decisions.rationale: Jesus uses 'เจ้า' for the woman — same as for healed-paralytic (Mark 2:5) and demoniac (Mark 5:19), but here colored by the 'ลูกหญิง' (daughter) address — making it warmly paternal rather than hierarchically distant. Per glossary: divine-to-human 'เจ้า' is the standard Markan register.

translation.notes: DAUGHTER: only Gospel instance of Jesus addressing a woman as 'Daughter.' Names the anonymous woman INTO family. FAITH-SAVES: σέσωκεν (perf.) in Mark — your faith has-saved-you-and-keeps-saving. Same construction at Mark 10:52 (Bartimaeus), Luke 7:50 (sinful woman), 17:19 (leper). Mark's σῴζω-formula intentionally fuses healing + salvation. SHALOM-BENEDICTION: 'go in peace' = the Hebrew lekh leshalom — Jesus dismisses her with the formal Hebrew farewell-blessing, completing her restoration not just physically but socially and ritually (her rejoining the community is sealed by the rabbi-figure's blessing). CONTRAST WITH JAIRUS: while Jesus pauses for this 12-year-suffering woman, Jairus' 12-year-old daughter dies (v.35). The intercalation forces the question: was the delay costly? Mark's answer (v.36): not for those who believe.
translation.thai_summary: นี่เป็นครั้งเดียวในพระกิตติคุณทั้งสี่ที่พระเยซูทรงเรียกผู้หญิงว่า 'ลูกหญิง' — นางไม่มีชื่อในเรื่อง แต่พระเยซูรับนางเข้าในครอบครัวของพระองค์ คำว่า 'หายโรคแล้ว' (σέσωκεν) มีรากเดียวกับ 'รอดแล้ว' — การรักษาทางกายเป็นสัญลักษณ์ของความรอดที่ลึกกว่า

greek: Ἔτι αὐτοῦ λαλοῦντος ἔρχονται ἀπὸ τοῦ ἀρχισυναγώγου λέγοντες ὅτι Ἡ θυγάτηρ σου ἀπέθανεν· τί ἔτι σκύλλεις τὸν διδάσκαλον;
bsb_english: While He was still speaking, messengers from the house of Jairus arrived and said, "Your daughter is dead; why bother the Teacher anymore?"

translation.thai: ขณะที่พระองค์ยังตรัสอยู่ มีคนมาจากบ้านของนายธรรมศาลา บอกว่า 'ลูกสาวของท่านสิ้นใจแล้ว จะรบกวนพระอาจารย์อีกทำไม?'

key_decisions.thai: ขณะที่พระองค์ยังตรัสอยู่
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW figs-explicit: Jesus is still speaking the v.34 benediction when the news arrives. Thai 'ขณะที่...ยังตรัสอยู่' (while...still royal-speaking) preserves the INTERRUPTION-TIMING. Royal 'ตรัส.'

key_decisions.thai: มีคนมาจากบ้านของนายธรรมศาลา
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW writing-pronouns + figs-metonymy: 'they come from the synagogue-ruler' = from his HOUSE (metonymy). Thai 'มีคน...มาจากบ้านของ' (there-are-people...come-from house-of) makes the metonymy explicit. Historic present preserved as narrative present.

key_decisions.thai: ลูกสาวของท่านสิ้นใจแล้ว
key_decisions.rationale: Direct quotation. Aorist ἀπέθανεν 'has died.' Thai 'ลูกสาวของท่านสิ้นใจแล้ว' (your-daughter has-passed-away) — 'สิ้นใจ' (exhale-the-last-breath) the same dignified euphemism as Jairus' 'ใกล้จะสิ้นใจ' in v.23, now realized: she who was 'near to last-breath' has now 'last-breath-completed.' The messengers address Jairus formally — they use 'ท่าน' (respectful-2nd-person) for him as a synagogue-ruler.

key_decisions.greek: τί ἔτι σκύλλεις τὸν διδάσκαλον; (rhet. q.)
key_decisions.thai: จะรบกวนพระอาจารย์อีกทำไม?
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW figs-rquestion + figs-explicit: rhetorical 'why bother the teacher anymore' implying 'don't bother him — there's nothing more he can do for a corpse.' σκύλλω = 'trouble, harass.' Thai 'รบกวน' (disturb) standard. 'พระอาจารย์' (royal-teacher) — 'พระ' honorific marks the messengers' acknowledgment of Jesus' status as a respected rabbi-teacher, even as their question implies his help is now useless.
