translation.notes: INTERRUPTION TIMING: Jesus' last word to the woman is 'be healed' — and at that exact moment, news of the death arrives. Mark's structure has the daughter's death overlap precisely with the woman's full healing. The narrative gut-punch: was this delay worth it? IMPLIED ASSUMPTION: the messengers (and presumably Jairus) believe death is the final boundary of Jesus' power. Disease can be healed; death is irreversible. This sets up Jesus' counter-statement in v.36.

greek: ὁ δὲ Ἰησοῦς παρακούσας τὸν λόγον λαλούμενον λέγει τῷ ἀρχισυναγώγῳ· Μὴ φοβοῦ, μόνον πίστευε.
bsb_english: But Jesus overheard their conversation and said to Jairus, "Do not be afraid; just believe."

translation.thai: แต่พระเยซูทรงได้ยินถ้อยคำที่พวกเขาพูด ก็ตรัสแก่นายธรรมศาลาว่า 'อย่ากลัวเลย จงเชื่อเท่านั้น'

key_decisions.thai: ทรงได้ยิน
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW v.36 ambiguity: παρακούω can mean (1) 'overhear' (listen alongside, hear something not directed at oneself) or (2) 'ignore, disregard' (refuse to heed). Both readings appear in the manuscript tradition. Most modern English versions (BSB, NIV, ESV) take 'overhear'; some commentators argue 'ignore' fits Mark's bold-Jesus characterization (refusing to accept the messengers' verdict). Thai 'ทรงได้ยิน' (royal-hear) preserves the basic 'overhear' reading; the additional resolve to act-against is shown by the 'แต่' (but) opening and the imperatives that follow. Royal 'ทรง' for Jesus' perception.

key_decisions.thai: ถ้อยคำที่พวกเขาพูด
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW figs-metonymy + figs-activepassive: 'the word being spoken' = what was said. Thai 'ถ้อยคำที่พวกเขาพูด' (the-words that-they-spoke) — active formation more natural in Thai.

key_decisions.thai: ตรัสแก่นายธรรมศาลา
key_decisions.rationale: Historic present (per uW v.36). Royal 'ตรัส.' Note: Jesus does NOT address the messengers; he addresses Jairus directly, bypassing the messengers' implied counsel.

key_decisions.greek: Μὴ φοβοῦ, μόνον πίστευε (pres. impv. — ongoing)
key_decisions.thai: อย่ากลัวเลย จงเชื่อเท่านั้น
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW figs-explicit: 'believe' implies trust IN JESUS — specifically that Jesus can save Jairus' daughter. Two present imperatives: 'stop fearing' (ongoing prohibition) + 'keep believing' (ongoing positive command). Thai 'อย่ากลัวเลย' (don't fear-particle 'เลย' for emphasis) + 'จงเชื่อเท่านั้น' (must-believe only) — 'เท่านั้น' (only) preserves μόνον's focusing force: faith is the one thing required.

translation.notes: INVERSION: where the messengers said 'don't bother' (despair-counsel), Jesus says 'don't fear, just believe' (faith-counsel). The intercalation now pays off: the woman's faith-saved-her becomes the model for Jairus. Jesus' delay was not negligence; the delay BECOMES the lesson. PRESENT IMPERATIVES: 'keep on believing' — Mark's grammar implies sustained, not momentary, trust. Faith here is not a one-time decision but an ongoing posture as the body of his daughter waits.

greek: καὶ οὐκ ἀφῆκεν οὐδένα μετ' αὐτοῦ συνακολουθῆσαι εἰ μὴ τὸν Πέτρον καὶ Ἰάκωβον καὶ Ἰωάννην τὸν ἀδελφὸν Ἰακώβου.
bsb_english: And He did not allow anyone to accompany Him except Peter, James, and John the brother of James.

translation.thai: พระองค์ไม่ทรงอนุญาตให้ผู้ใดติดตามพระองค์ไป ยกเว้นเปโตร ยากอบ และยอห์นน้องชายของยากอบ

key_decisions.thai: ไม่ทรงอนุญาตให้ผู้ใด...ยกเว้น
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW grammar-connect-exceptions: 'allowed no one...except.' Thai 'ไม่ทรง...ผู้ใด...ยกเว้น' (not-royal...anyone...except) preserves the exception-clause structure. Jesus deliberately winnows the entourage — the crowd that just pressed in (v.31) is now barred from this private moment. He selects the inner three.

key_decisions.greek: τὸν Πέτρον καὶ Ἰάκωβον καὶ Ἰωάννην τὸν ἀδελφὸν Ἰακώβου
key_decisions.thai: เปโตร ยากอบ และยอห์นน้องชายของยากอบ
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW translate-kinship: Mark calls John 'brother of James' — typically suggests John is younger (cf. Mark 1:19 where they are called together, James first). Thai 'น้องชาย' (younger brother) preserves the kinship + age implication, consistent with Mark 1:19 / 3:17 precedent. The three names: standard Thai biblical transliteration. Ben hasn't translated Mark 1 in this project's render here, so I follow standard Thai Christian: เปโตร, ยากอบ, ยอห์น. THE INNER THREE: also present at Transfiguration (9:2) and Gethsemane (14:33) — Mark's signature select-three.

translation.notes: INNER THREE: Peter, James, John — the same three at the Transfiguration (9:2) and Gethsemane (14:33). Jesus' select-witness pattern across Mark's most theologically-loaded private moments: glory, agony, resurrection. The raising here is treated as on the same level — a moment requiring select witness, not crowd-spectacle. Per uW v.37 figs-explicit: Jairus also went, of course. The exclusion is of OTHERS — the press of strangers.
translation.thai_summary: เปโตร ยากอบ และยอห์น — สามสาวกชั้นในนี้จะปรากฏอีกที่การจำแลงพระกาย (9:2) และที่สวนเกทเสมนี (14:33) ทั้งสามเหตุการณ์เป็นจุดสำคัญทางเทววิทยาของมาระโก: ความรุ่งโรจน์ ความทุกข์ และการคืนชีพ

greek: καὶ ἔρχονται εἰς τὸν οἶκον τοῦ ἀρχισυναγώγου, καὶ θεωρεῖ θόρυβον καὶ κλαίοντας καὶ ἀλαλάζοντας πολλά,
bsb_english: When they arrived at the house of the synagogue leader, Jesus saw the commotion and the people weeping and wailing loudly.

translation.thai: พวกเขามาถึงบ้านของนายธรรมศาลา พระองค์ทอดพระเนตรเห็นความวุ่นวาย ผู้คนกำลังร้องไห้คร่ำครวญเสียงดัง

key_decisions.thai: มาถึง...ทอดพระเนตรเห็น
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW v.38 historic presents — vivid arrival scene. Thai uses simple narrative verbs. Royal 'ทอดพระเนตรเห็น' for Jesus' sight.

key_decisions.greek: θόρυβον καὶ κλαίοντας καὶ ἀλαλάζοντας πολλά
key_decisions.thai: ความวุ่นวาย ผู้คนกำลังร้องไห้คร่ำครวญเสียงดัง
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW figs-hendiadys + figs-doublet: 'commotion' is constituted by 'weeping and much wailing.' Thai compresses while preserving the auditory/emotional intensity. 'ความวุ่นวาย' (chaos/commotion) for θόρυβος; 'ร้องไห้คร่ำครวญ' (weep-lament — paired Thai verb for funeral-mourning) for κλαίοντας + ἀλαλάζοντας; 'เสียงดัง' (loud-voice) for πολλά (adverbial 'much/loudly'). NOTE: ancient Jewish funerals included professional mourners (often hired women) who wailed loudly — even a poor family was expected to provide at least two flute-players and one wailing-woman per Mishnah Ketubot 4.4. The κλαίοντας + ἀλαλάζοντας likely includes both genuine grief and ritual performance. The next verse's mocking laughter (v.40 κατεγέλων) makes more sense if some of these mourners were professionals quickly switched from grief to derision.

translation.notes: FUNERARY CULTURE: ancient Jewish funerals required professional mourners and musicians; the noise level was substantial. Mishnah Ketubot 4.4 prescribes minimum even for the poorest. The shift from wailing to mockery in v.40 is consistent with hired-mourner behavior — performance, not grief. ἀλαλάζω is onomatopoeic ('alalai!') — a ritualized mourning-cry. Mark's auditory tableau is high-decibel chaos.

greek: καὶ εἰσελθὼν λέγει αὐτοῖς· Τί θορυβεῖσθε καὶ κλαίετε; τὸ παιδίον οὐκ ἀπέθανεν ἀλλὰ καθεύδει.
bsb_english: He went inside and asked, "Why all this commotion and weeping? The child is not dead, but asleep."

translation.thai: พระองค์เสด็จเข้าไปและตรัสกับพวกเขาว่า 'ทำไมพวกท่านจึงวุ่นวายและร้องไห้กันเล่า? เด็กน้อยมิได้ตาย เพียงแต่นอนหลับอยู่เท่านั้น'

key_decisions.thai: เสด็จเข้าไปและตรัส
key_decisions.rationale: Royal 'เสด็จ' + 'ตรัส' for Jesus. Historic present preserved as narrative.

key_decisions.thai: ทำไมพวกท่านจึงวุ่นวายและร้องไห้กันเล่า?
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW figs-rquestion + figs-yousingular (note: PLURAL 'you' since addressing many): rebuke-form question. Thai 'ทำไม...จึง...เล่า' (why...so...particle) preserves the rebuke-question form. 'พวกท่าน' (plural respectful — Jesus addressing the assembled mourners politely as adults). 'กัน' (collective particle) reinforces the plural addressing.

key_decisions.greek: τὸ παιδίον οὐκ ἀπέθανεν ἀλλὰ καθεύδει
key_decisions.thai: เด็กน้อยมิได้ตาย เพียงแต่นอนหลับอยู่เท่านั้น
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW figs-explicit: παιδίον = young child, here a 12-year-old (v.42). Thai 'เด็กน้อย' (small child — affectionate diminutive) preserves Mark's tender vocabulary. Per uW chapter intro on resurrection: Jesus' use of 'sleep' is not a denial of death but a euphemism reclaiming death's power — for Jesus, death is reversible like sleep. NT precedent: John 11:11 (Lazarus 'has fallen asleep'); 1 Cor 15:6 (the saints 'fall asleep'); 1 Thess 4:13–14. Thai 'นอนหลับ' (sleep) standard; 'เพียงแต่...เท่านั้น' (only...only) preserves the strong contrast. Jesus' theological challenge: death is not what you think it is.

translation.notes: SLEEP-EUPHEMISM for death — became standard NT vocabulary (John 11:11; 1 Cor 11:30, 15:6, 51; 1 Thess 4:13–14). For Jesus, death is to him what sleep is to a parent — easily undone by his word. The mourners' mockery (v.40) reveals they hear the saying literally and find it absurd; the disciples will hear it and not yet understand (until 9:9, then fully at the Resurrection). This verse is one of Mark's most theologically loaded — death is recategorized.
translation.thai_summary: คำว่า 'หลับ' เป็นสำนวนที่พันธสัญญาใหม่ใช้สำหรับความตายของผู้เชื่อ (ยอห์น 11:11; 1 โครินธ์ 15:51-52; 1 เธสะโลนิกา 4:13-14) สำหรับพระเยซู ความตายเป็นเหมือนการหลับที่สามารถปลุกให้ตื่นได้ด้วยคำพูดของพระองค์

greek: καὶ κατεγέλων αὐτοῦ. αὐτὸς δὲ ἐκβαλὼν πάντας παραλαμβάνει τὸν πατέρα τοῦ παιδίου καὶ τὴν μητέρα καὶ τοὺς μετ' αὐτοῦ, καὶ εἰσπορεύεται ὅπου ἦν τὸ παιδίον·
bsb_english: And they laughed at Him. After He had put them all outside, He took the child's father and mother and His own companions, and went in to see the child.

translation.thai: พวกเขาก็พากันหัวเราะเยาะพระองค์ แต่พระองค์ทรงไล่คนทั้งหมดออกไป แล้วทรงพาบิดาและมารดาของเด็ก กับผู้ที่อยู่กับพระองค์ เข้าไปยังที่ที่เด็กนอนอยู่

key_decisions.thai: พากันหัวเราะเยาะ
key_decisions.rationale: καταγελάω = 'mock, ridicule, laugh-down at' (κατά intensive). Imperfect = sustained mocking. Thai 'พากันหัวเราะเยาะ' (collectively-laugh-mockingly) preserves both the social-collective and the contempt. The instant pivot from wailing to mockery exposes the funeral noise as performance, not grief.

key_decisions.thai: แต่พระองค์ทรงไล่คนทั้งหมดออกไป
key_decisions.rationale: ἐκβάλλω = throw out, expel — same verb used for Jesus casting out demons (Mark 1:34, 39 etc.). Thai 'ทรงไล่...ออกไป' (royal-drive...out) preserves the forceful expulsion. The mockers are removed before the miracle — they have disqualified themselves from witness by their unbelief.

key_decisions.greek: παραλαμβάνει τὸν πατέρα...καὶ τὴν μητέρα καὶ τοὺς μετ' αὐτοῦ (hist. pres.)
key_decisions.thai: ทรงพาบิดาและมารดาของเด็ก กับผู้ที่อยู่กับพระองค์
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW v.40 figs-explicit: 'those with him' = the three disciples (Peter, James, John from v.37). Thai 'ทรงพา' (royal-take/lead). 'บิดาและมารดา' (father and mother) — formal Thai for parents in dignified contexts. 'ผู้ที่อยู่กับพระองค์' (those-who-are-with-him) for τοὺς μετ' αὐτοῦ — preserves the inner-circle reference.

key_decisions.greek: εἰσπορεύεται ὅπου ἦν τὸ παιδίον (hist. pres.)
key_decisions.thai: เข้าไปยังที่ที่เด็กนอนอยู่
key_decisions.rationale: εἰσπορεύομαι = enter (durative-aspect 'go-into'). Thai 'เข้าไปยัง' (go-into-toward) preserves the directional motion. 'ที่ที่เด็กนอนอยู่' (the-place-where-the-child-lies) — supplied 'นอนอยู่' (is-lying) for clarity, picking up the v.39 sleep-imagery.

translation.notes: SCENE NARROWED to five: Jesus, Jairus, his wife, Peter, James, John — and the dead child. The mockers are removed, signaling that this miracle requires the right witnesses. Mark's Jesus does not perform spectacles for skeptics. Cf. 6:5 — even Jesus could 'do no mighty work there' in Nazareth because of unbelief. Faith is the condition of witness.

greek: καὶ κρατήσας τῆς χειρὸς τοῦ παιδίου λέγει αὐτῇ· Ταλιθα κουμ, ὅ ἐστιν μεθερμηνευόμενον· Τὸ κοράσιον, σοὶ λέγω, ἔγειρε.
bsb_english: Taking her by the hand, Jesus said, "Talitha koum!" which means, "Little girl, I say to you, get up!"

translation.thai: พระองค์ทรงจับมือเด็กนั้น และตรัสกับเธอว่า 'ทาลิธา คูม' ซึ่งแปลว่า 'แม่หนูเอ๋ย เราสั่งเจ้าว่า จงลุกขึ้นเถิด'

key_decisions.thai: ทรงจับมือเด็กนั้น
key_decisions.rationale: κρατέω + gen. = take hold of, seize. Royal 'ทรงจับ' (royal-grasp) + 'มือเด็ก' (child's-hand). Per Lev 21:1–4, contact with a corpse rendered a priest unclean; Jesus, like with the bleeding woman, reverses the impurity-flow — instead of being defiled, he heals. Skin-on-skin contact with a corpse-body, deliberate.

key_decisions.thai: ทาลิธา คูม
key_decisions.rationale: HAPAX (Aramaic transliteration in NT — only here): ταλιθα = Aramaic טליתא 'little girl' (fem. of טליא); κουμ = Aramaic קום 'arise' (impv.). Per uW translate-transliterate: Mark transliterates Jesus' Aramaic word and immediately translates for his Greek-speaking audience. Thai 'ทาลิธา คูม' (Thai-script transliteration of the Aramaic — preserves the foreign-word effect). PRESERVING THE ARAMAIC: Mark's gesture preserves Jesus' actual spoken language — an authenticity-marker rare in the Greek gospels (also Mark 7:34 ἐφφαθα; 14:36 αββα; 15:34 ἐλωι ἐλωι λεμα σαβαχθανι). For first-century readers and modern, hearing Jesus' actual word adds intimacy. NOTE: SBLGNT punctuates as two separate words (Ταλιθα κουμ); some MSS read κουμι (κούμι — fem. impv.). The κουμ form is grammatically masculine but is here used as an indeclinable address. Translation tradition retains 'koum.'

key_decisions.thai: ซึ่งแปลว่า
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW figs-activepassive: 'which is being translated' (passive) = 'which means.' Thai 'ซึ่งแปลว่า' (which-translates-as) standard. Mark editorializes for his Greek audience — the act of translation is itself preserved in the text.

key_decisions.thai: แม่หนูเอ๋ย เราสั่งเจ้าว่า จงลุกขึ้นเถิด
key_decisions.rationale: κοράσιον = 'little girl' (diminutive of κόρη). σοὶ λέγω = 'to you I say' (emphatic 'I' implied by Aramaic). ἔγειρε = 'arise!' (pres. impv.). Thai 'แม่หนูเอ๋ย' (girl-vocative — affectionate Thai address for a young female child, equivalent to 'sweetie / dear little one' but standard biblical Thai usage); 'เราสั่งเจ้าว่า' (I-command-you-that — 'เรา' for Jesus' royal self-reference, 'เจ้า' for the child as divine-to-human). 'จงลุกขึ้นเถิด' (must-arise-particle) — 'เถิด' adds gentle imperative force, fitting paternal address.

key_decisions.thai: ทาลิธา คูม
key_decisions.rationale: Following Mark's deliberate strategy: transliterate then translate. The Aramaic word is preserved as a foreign word in the Thai text — modern Thai readers will recognize this as 'a word in the language Jesus spoke.'

translation.notes: ARAMAIC HAPAX: Ταλιθα κουμ — Mark preserves Jesus' actual spoken Aramaic, then immediately translates for his Greek audience. The fact that Mark preserves these moments (also at 7:34, 14:36, 15:34) suggests an oral-tradition authenticity-marker — these words were remembered as Jesus actually spoke them. THEOLOGICAL SIGNIFICANCE: Jesus speaks tenderly, in his and the child's native tongue, into death itself — and death obeys Aramaic. ECHO of GREAT EZEKIEL/PROPHETIC RESURRECTION: Ezek 37:9 LXX ἐλθὲ τὸ πνεῦμα...καὶ ἀνάστησον — but Jesus needs no formula, just a familiar address.
translation.thai_summary: 'ทาลิธา คูม' เป็นภาษาอารามาอิก ซึ่งเป็นภาษาที่พระเยซูตรัสจริง มาระโกเก็บรักษาคำต้นฉบับไว้แล้วแปลให้ผู้อ่านภาษากรีก — เช่นเดียวกับที่ 7:34 (เอฟาธา), 14:36 (อับบา), 15:34 (เอโลอี) เป็นเครื่องหมายของประเพณีปากเปล่าที่จำคำของพระเยซูไว้อย่างแม่นยำ

greek: καὶ εὐθὺς ἀνέστη τὸ κοράσιον καὶ περιεπάτει, ἦν γὰρ ἐτῶν δώδεκα. καὶ ἐξέστησαν εὐθὺς ἐκστάσει μεγάλῃ.
bsb_english: Immediately the girl got up and began to walk around (she was twelve years old). And at once they were utterly astounded.

translation.thai: ในทันใดนั้น เด็กหญิงนั้นก็ลุกขึ้นและเดินไปมา เพราะเธอมีอายุสิบสองปี และพวกเขาทั้งหลายก็ตกตะลึงด้วยความอัศจรรย์ใจอย่างยิ่ง

key_decisions.thai: ในทันใดนั้น...ลุกขึ้น
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW figs-explicit: 'rose up' = both came back to life AND stood up. ἀνίστημι is also the standard NT resurrection verb (cf. Mark 8:31, 9:9, 16:6). Thai 'ลุกขึ้น' (rise-up) carries both physical-rising and resurrection-rising senses naturally. εὐθύς = Mark's signature 'in-the-instant.'

key_decisions.thai: เดินไปมา
key_decisions.rationale: Imperfect = ongoing walking. Thai 'เดินไปมา' (walk-back-and-forth) preserves the durative — she's not just standing, she's moving around the room, demonstrating life. The narrator captures the witnesses' eyes following her.

key_decisions.thai: เพราะเธอมีอายุสิบสองปี
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW figs-infostructure + figs-idiom + grammar-connect-words-phrases: γάρ explains why she could walk (old enough to walk normally — not a toddler). The reader also gets the 12-years number that ties to the woman's 12-year affliction (v.25). Thai 'เพราะเธอมีอายุสิบสองปี' (because-she-has-age 12-years) — 'มีอายุ' standard Thai for stating someone's age.

key_decisions.thai: ตกตะลึงด้วยความอัศจรรย์ใจอย่างยิ่ง
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW figs-idiom + figs-abstractnouns: cognate-accusative-style construction 'they were-astonished with great astonishment' — Hebraic intensification (cf. LXX Gen 27:33 ἐξέστη...ἔκστασιν μεγάλην). ἐξίστημι/ἔκστασις family appeared at Mark 2:12 (ἐξίστασθαι), 3:21 (ἐξέστη — there meaning 'beside himself'), 6:51, etc. Thai 'ตกตะลึง' (be-stunned) + 'ด้วยความอัศจรรย์ใจอย่างยิ่ง' (with-amazement-very-great) preserves the cognate-intensifying force without forcing literal verbal duplication. Mark stacks two intensifiers (εὐθύς + cognate-dative-amplification) to mark the witnesses' overwhelming response — words fail.

key_decisions.thai: ตกตะลึงด้วยความอัศจรรย์ใจอย่างยิ่ง
key_decisions.rationale: Per LXX-influenced Hebraism: verb + cognate-noun = intensification. Thai must use a different intensification (adverb 'อย่างยิ่ง' rather than verbal repetition) — verbal cognate-doubling sounds clumsy in Thai but adverbial-intensification is natural.

translation.notes: COGNATE ACCUSATIVE: ἐξέστησαν...ἐκστάσει μεγάλῃ — Hebraism for utter astonishment. Mark's grammatical extravagance shows that words have run out for what was witnessed. RESURRECTION VOCABULARY: ἀνίστημι is the standard NT resurrection verb — the same word that will be used for Jesus' own resurrection (Mark 8:31, 9:9, 9:31, 10:34, 16:6). The girl's rising from her bed is a foretaste, in miniature, of Easter morning. NUMERICAL CLOSE: 12 years (girl's age) ↔ 12 years (woman's affliction in v.25). The intercalation is bound by 12. Mark's structural elegance: two women, both '12,' both restored by faith-touch.

greek: καὶ διεστείλατο αὐτοῖς πολλὰ ἵνα μηδεὶς γνοῖ τοῦτο, καὶ εἶπεν δοθῆναι αὐτῇ φαγεῖν.
bsb_english: Then Jesus gave strict orders that no one should know about this, and He told them to give her something to eat.

translation.thai: พระองค์ทรงกำชับพวกเขาอย่างแข็งขันว่า อย่าให้ผู้ใดรู้เรื่องนี้ และทรงสั่งให้นำอาหารมาให้เด็กนั้นกิน

key_decisions.thai: ทรงกำชับพวกเขาอย่างแข็งขันว่า
key_decisions.rationale: διαστέλλομαι = 'order strictly, charge.' πολλά adverbial 'much, strongly.' Royal 'ทรงกำชับ' (royal-strict-instruct — 'กำชับ' is the Thai verb for repeated/emphatic instruction); 'อย่างแข็งขัน' (firmly/strongly) for πολλά. Mark's signature messianic-secret command (cf. 1:34, 1:44, 3:12, 7:36, 8:30, 9:9) — RESTORED: contrast with Mark 5:19 where Jesus commanded the Gerasene to PROCLAIM. The difference is geography and audience: Decapolis (Gentile) → publicize; Galilean Jewish home → silence. Mark preserves both impulses.

key_decisions.thai: อย่าให้ผู้ใดรู้เรื่องนี้
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW figs-quotations: could be direct quote 'No one must know about this.' We keep indirect for Greek-following. Thai 'อย่าให้ผู้ใดรู้' (not-allow anyone to-know) preserves the prohibition. SECRECY-PROBLEM: how could this be kept secret? Funeral was already public (mourners present); the girl's resurrection would be visible the moment she walked outside. Mark's command may be aspirational rather than enforceable — or it may pertain only to the manner-of-the-raising (the inner-circle witness alone knew Jesus said 'Talitha koum,' touched her, etc.).

key_decisions.thai: ทรงสั่งให้นำอาหารมาให้เด็กนั้นกิน
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW figs-quotations + figs-activepassive: 'said for it to be given to her to eat' — order form. Thai 'ทรงสั่งให้นำอาหารมาให้เด็กนั้นกิน' (royal-order-that bring-food to-give-the-child to-eat). Royal 'ทรง' for Jesus' authoritative command. PRACTICAL TENDERNESS: Jesus thinks of the immediate post-resurrection reality — the girl is alive, hungry, depleted. He cares about meals. Mark's vivid detail: the same hands that just commanded death to release her now arrange dinner. The miracle ends in domesticity.

translation.notes: MESSIANIC SECRET: contrast Mark 5:19 (publish) with 5:43 (silence). The difference is regional/ethnic — in Jewish Galilee Jesus avoids fueling political-messianic expectations; in Gentile Decapolis the testimony cannot be coopted. Mark's geography sets the rule. PASTORAL TENDERNESS: the chapter ends not with theological declaration but with 'feed the child' — Jesus the resurrector is also Jesus the housekeeper. The miracle returns to ordinary life. CHAPTER CLOSE: Mark 5 has moved from a man living among tombs (death-territory) to a girl freshly raised from death (life-victory) — bookended by triumph over death's dominion. Three lives transformed by faith-encounter: a demoniac, a hemorrhaging woman, a dead child. Mark's Christology by accumulated miracle.
translation.thai_summary: ตรงข้ามกับ 5:19 ที่พระเยซูทรงสั่งให้ชายคนนั้นประกาศในเขตเดคาโปลิส ที่นี่ในแคว้นยิวพระองค์ทรงสั่งเงียบ ความแตกต่างอยู่ที่บริบท: ในแคว้นกาลิลี การประกาศเรื่องอัศจรรย์อาจปลุกการคาดหวังพระเมสสิยาห์ทางการเมือง ในเขตคนต่างชาตินั้นไม่ใช่ความเสี่ยงเดียวกัน
